RESULTS	People who have migrated recently have a less varied diet and may be proned to suffer nutritional deficiences.

###11261812
OBJECTIVE	To investigate the immediate causes of death, in autopsied demented and non-demented elderly.
METHODS	Retrospective clinicopathologic correlations.
METHODS	Acute and intermediate care geriatric hospital.
POPULATION	342 hospitalized demented and non-demented elderly (mean age 84.94 +/- 6.9 years) who underwent consecutive postmortem examinations: 120 demented patients with either vascular dementia (VaD, n = 34), mixed dementia (MD, n = 65) or Alzheimer's disease (AD, n=21) neuropathologically confirmed and 222 nondemented elderly.
RESULTS	Primary causes of death were similar in both demented and non-demented patients; the commonest were cardiovascular disease and bronchopneumonia.
RESULTS	Cardiac causes of death and especially cardiac failure were more frequent in VaD than in AD or MD (respectively P = 0.027 and 0.005).
RESULTS	Dementia was an underlying but never a primary cause of death.
CONCLUSIONS	Immediate causes of death are similar in elderly demented and non-demented patients.

###11261757
BACKGROUND	Erythema multiforme (EM) is a vesiculobullous disorder with variable manifestations which predominantly affects the skin.
BACKGROUND	It is regarded as a hypersensitivity disorder which is triggered by multiple factors such as infection, drugs and food.
BACKGROUND	Varicella zoster virus (VZV) has rarely been reported as an etiological agent, despite its high incidence as a pathogen in childhood.
POPULATION	We describe two children in whom EM preceded VZV infection.
POPULATION	In the first, a 5-year-old boy, EM was followed 3 days later by a classical disseminated varicella eruption.
POPULATION	The diagnosis was reached by clinical, epidemiological and serological means.
POPULATION	The second patient was a 13-year-old boy with EM which was followed 2 weeks later by Ramsay-Hunt syndrome.
POPULATION	The diagnosis was confirmed by skin biopsy, positive serology and viral culture.
CONCLUSIONS	The association of EM and VZV infection is probably more common than reported.
CONCLUSIONS	In clinical cases of EM, VZV should be included in the list of possible causative agents.

###11257737
BACKGROUND	The use of antibiotics in uncomplicated appendicitis in children, remains the area of controversy.
BACKGROUND	The aim of the study was to assess the necessity of antibiotic administration in nonperforated appendicitis in children.
METHODS	The design of the study was a double blind randomized controlled trial, with a follow-up of 4 to 20 months.
METHODS	Surgical Department in a University Pediatric Hospital.
POPULATION	One hundred and eighty seven out of 249 children subjected to emergency appendectomies met the inclusion criteria, with 35 eligible but not included in the study.
POPULATION	The remaining 152 patients were randomized; 41 had complicated appendicitis, 3 other diagnosis, 108 were analyzed within 3 study groups: 1 (n = 31) no antibiotic, 2 (n = 41) one dose, 3 (n = 36) 5-day course.
POPULATION	Open appendectomy was a surgical procedure and Ceftriaxone 1.0 g i.v. was administered.
POPULATION	Investigated parameters were: body temperature, WBC, bowel sounds, wound healing, recovery and morbidity.
RESULTS	Valid outcome data were available for 90 of 108 randomized patients.
RESULTS	Protocols of 18 children due to fever > 39 degrees C, upper airway infection or allergy were disclosed.
RESULTS	In the remaining 90 children, there were no differences in WBC and oral feeding between groups 1 (n = 24), 2 (n = 35) and 3 (n = 31).
RESULTS	Group 1 and 2 had a higher mean temperature on day 1 post-op, without any clinical significance.
RESULTS	A higher mean temperature was noted on day 5 post-op in group 1, due to wound infection in one patient.
RESULTS	There were no intraabdominal abscesses.
RESULTS	The only other complications were 2 adhesion small bowel obstructions (in groups 1 and 2 each).
CONCLUSIONS	Routine use of antibiotics in nonperforated appendicitis in children is not necessary.

###11256871
BACKGROUND	The normal life expectancy of survivors of early-stage breast cancer (BCS) underscores the need to address long-term quality of life issues in these women.
BACKGROUND	Sexual dysfunction persists after breast cancer treatment, despite recovery in other domains.
OBJECTIVE	To examine associations between a broad array of characteristics and sexuality in BCS.
POPULATION	Sixty-one postmenopausal BCS who were participants in a randomized, controlled trial of nonhormonal interventions for menopause symptoms and who had a partnered, intimate relationship.
METHODS	Cross-sectional analysis of baseline trial data.
METHODS	Outcomes were standardized scales of sexual interest, dysfunction, and satisfaction.
METHODS	Candidate predictors included demographic, anatomical, medical, psychological, sociocultural, and hormonal characteristics.
METHODS	Forward, stepwise regression was used.
RESULTS	Relationship quality, vaginal discomfort, education, and hot flashes were each associated with two of the three domains of sexuality assessed.
RESULTS	Ten other factors entered predictive models: age, time since diagnosis, breast conservation, comorbidity, urinary incontinence, perceived health, body image, bioavailable testosterone, luteinizing hormone, and sex hormone binding globulin.
RESULTS	Each of these 10 factors was associated with only one sexuality domain.
CONCLUSIONS	In this small sample of BCS, we found multiple correlates of sexuality.
CONCLUSIONS	Most seem to impact uniquely on individual domains of sexual function.
CONCLUSIONS	Several characteristics are modifiable and could be targets for intervention.

###11255472
OBJECTIVE	To present guidelines for the management of an orbital subperiosteal abscess (SPA) in children and to assess the efficacy and safety of transnasal endoscopic drainage of an orbital SPA.
METHODS	Tertiary care children's hospital.
POPULATION	Nineteen patients treated for an SPA between July 1997 and December 1999.
POPULATION	The age of the patients ranged from 17 months to 14 years (mean, 6 years).
POPULATION	The male-female ratio was 10:9.
POPULATION	Treatment modalities included transnasal endoscopic drainage (n = 11), external drainage (n = 3), and intravenous antibiotics alone (n = 5).
RESULTS	Bilateral pansinusitis was the most common cause.
RESULTS	All patients received an initial trial of intravenous antibiotics.
RESULTS	Based on the Fisher exact test, no statistically significant differences were detected for age, sex, presence of gaze restriction, and radiographic findings.
RESULTS	Based on multiple logistic regression, degree of proptosis was the only significant multivariate predictor of surgery (P =.003).
RESULTS	The estimated probability of surgery was 6% when there was no proptosis, and 92% for 2 mm of proptosis.
RESULTS	The location of the SPA determined the route of surgical drainage.
RESULTS	Eleven patients with a medially based SPA underwent drainage via the transnasal endoscopic approach, and 3 with a superior SPA underwent drainage externally.
RESULTS	The external approach was associated with a longer hospital stay (median, 7 days) than either the endoscopic or the intravenous antibiotic approach (median, 5 days).

###11254834
OBJECTIVE	To undertake neuropsychologic testing within 18 hours of cardiac surgery after fast-track anesthesia.
METHODS	Prospective study.
METHODS	University hospital, single center.
POPULATION	Fifty patients undergoing first-time elective coronary artery surgery.
INTERVENTION	A neuropsychologic test battery was administered preoperatively and 18 hours and 5 days after surgery.
RESULTS	Seven patients were withdrawn, and 9 patients did not attempt the postoperative tests (on both occasions) because of medical complications.
RESULTS	Thirty patients completed > or =4 tests at both postoperative occasions.
RESULTS	Of these, 9 patients (30%) showed a deficit in > or =2 tests at 18 hours postoperatively, and 3 (10%) showed a deficit at 5 days postoperatively.
CONCLUSIONS	In the absence of medical complications and despite the difficulties, early postoperative neuropsychologic testing is possible after fast-track anesthesia.
CONCLUSIONS	Such testing has the potential to more clearly define the course of cognitive decline after cardiac surgery.

###11253380
OBJECTIVE	To define the pathophysiologic substrate of gender differences in cognition and behavior in Alzheimer's disease (AD).
METHODS	Hemispheric regional cerebral blood flow (rCBF) was studied in a consecutive series of dementia patients using single photon emission computed tomography (SPECT).
POPULATION	Subjects included 300 outpatients who were studied with SPECT as part of a diagnostic evaluation for degenerative dementia or memory disorder.
METHODS	Based on qualitative descriptions by a radiologist, subjects were classified as having unilateral left, unilateral right, bilateral, or no perfusion defects.
METHODS	Semiquantitative analysis of SPECT images was also performed using region of interest radionuclide counts normalized to the cerebellum.
RESULTS	Among 174 females and 126 males, unilateral left hemisphere defects were found more commonly in women than men (24% vs 10%; chi 2 = 9.4; P = .009).
RESULTS	This observation was most significant for the 103 females and 62 males regarded as having probable AD (26% vs 8%; chi 2 = 9.3; P = .01).
RESULTS	In a multiple regression model of clinical variables, shorter duration of disease and female gender were significant independent predictors of the unilateral left hemisphere pattern among those with probable AD.
RESULTS	Age, family history, education, handedness, and severity of cognitive impairment were not significant contributors.
CONCLUSIONS	Women with AD exhibit greater heterogeneity in rCBF than men.
CONCLUSIONS	Asymmetry in rCBF occurs more often in women.
CONCLUSIONS	In some cases, this is related to relative preservation of right hemisphere function in women.

###11253257
OBJECTIVE	To determine whether there are gender differences in insulin-mediated glucose utilization and if sex hormones correlate with measures of insulin sensitivity in young adult African-Americans.
METHODS	Cross-sectional case (women)-control (men) study.
POPULATION	African-American men and women aged 27 to 35 years.
POPULATION	Excluded were known diabetics, individuals on antihypertensive therapy, and women taking exogenous estrogen preparations.
METHODS	Procedures included anthropometric and blood pressure measurement, oral glucose tolerance test, sex hormone assay, and euglycemic hyperinsulinemic clamp.
METHODS	Procedures for data analysis included two-way analysis of variance and Pearson's correlation coefficients.
RESULTS	Data were analyzed on 104 men and 142 women with a mean age of 31.5 years.
RESULTS	Insulin sensitivity was lower in women than in men.
RESULTS	When insulin-mediated glucose utilization was corrected for body fat, there was no gender difference in insulin sensitivity.
RESULTS	There was a significant correlation of androgen status with insulin sensitivity, but this relationship was divergent between men and women.
RESULTS	For men, the correlation between insulin sensitivity and free testosterone was positive (r = .36, P < .001).
RESULTS	For women, this correlation was negative (r = -.28, P = .001).
CONCLUSIONS	These data on young African-Americans demonstrate no gender differences in insulin sensitivity when glucose utilization is corrected for adipose mass.
CONCLUSIONS	Androgen status is significantly linked with insulin sensitivity, but the relationship is divergent in men and women.
CONCLUSIONS	Insulin resistance in young women is strongly associated with relative androgen excess, which may augment the risk for cardiovascular disease.

###11253248
OBJECTIVE	To assess gender and ethnic differences among teenagers in heart health behaviors, risk factors for coronary heart disease (CHD), and cardiovascular fitness.
METHODS	Observations consist of cross-sectional data collected prior to a school-based health promotion intervention program.
POPULATION	Teenage girls (N = 865) and boys (N = 497) from three New York City high schools.
POPULATION	The ethnic composition of this sample was 20% Asian-American, 40% African-American, 25% Hispanic, and 15% white.
METHODS	Subjects were compared on the following: height, weight, body mass index, percentage body fat, total cholesterol, blood pressure, heart health knowledge, family history, socioeconomic status, dietary habits, smoking, physical activity, and estimated aerobic capacity.
METHODS	Differences were assessed with independent t tests, analysis of variance, and chi-square statistical techniques.
RESULTS	Compared with girls, boys were more active and had higher estimated aerobic capacity, higher systolic blood pressure, and better self-perception of health.
RESULTS	Compared with boys, girls had higher cholesterol, percentage body fat, and heart health knowledge scores and ate fewer foods high in saturated fat, cholesterol, salt, and simple sugars.
RESULTS	Among girls, African-Americans had the highest blood pressure, cholesterol, body mass index, and intake of foods high in saturated fat, cholesterol, and sugar.
RESULTS	Among boys, Hispanics had the highest body mass index and percentage body fat and the lowest heart health knowledge scores.
RESULTS	White girls and white boys were the most frequent smokers.
CONCLUSIONS	Poor health behaviors and risk factors for CHD occurred frequently among urban teenagers.
CONCLUSIONS	In general, teenage girls had poorer health behaviors and a greater prevalence of risk factors than teenage boys, even though they scored better in heart health knowledge testing.
CONCLUSIONS	Ethnic comparisons revealed poorer health behaviors and higher prevalence of risk factors in African-American and Hispanic teens compared with white and Asian-American teens.
CONCLUSIONS	Results support the need for health promotion intervention among urban teenagers.

###11253236
OBJECTIVE	To determine why women have a higher mortality rate than men when undergoing coronary artery bypass grafting.
METHODS	Retrospective analysis of patients entered in a mandatory state database.
POPULATION	19,224 patients who underwent coronary artery bypass grafting in New York State in 1995.
METHODS	The authors evaluated data pertaining to 27 variables.
METHODS	They conducted univariate analysis using the Student t test for continuous variables and the chi-square test or the Fisher exact test for discrete data.
METHODS	They conducted multivariate analysis using a logistic regression model.
RESULTS	Analysis of body surface area revealed that smaller size was a risk factor for both women and men.
RESULTS	Analysis of age demonstrated increased risk for mortality in women in both older and younger subpopulations.
RESULTS	Other significant variables included a lesser degree of revascularization and less frequent use of the internal mammary artery in women.
CONCLUSIONS	Smaller size and advanced age alone do not explain why female gender is an independent risk factor for mortality from coronary artery bypass grafting.
CONCLUSIONS	Increased mortality is probably due to the fact that women have more comorbid conditions than men at the time of referral, perhaps because they are not being evaluated aggressively enough.

###11252860
OBJECTIVE	To estimate the magnitude of genetic and environmental factors on anxiety sensitivity by gender.
METHODS	Classic twins reared-together study design.
POPULATION	A community sample of 337 twin pairs, including 179 monozygotic (45 brother and 134 sister pairs) and 158 dizygotic (28 brother, 94 sister, and 36 brother-sister pairs).
METHODS	Twin pairs completed the Anxiety Sensitivity Index (ASI) using a postal survey design.
METHODS	The ASI is composed of three factors: (1) fear of anxiety-related somatic sensations; (2) fear of cognitive dyscontrol due to beliefs that sensations like depersonalization are signs of mental illness (e.g., fear of concentration problems); and (3) fear of publicly observable anxiety reactions (e.g., fear of trembling).
METHODS	Biometrical modeling techniques were used to estimate heritability of the ASI dimensions by gender.
RESULTS	ASI factors are heritable only in women, accounting for 37% to 48% of the total variance (median, 44.5%).
RESULTS	Environmental factors accounted for all the variability in men.
CONCLUSIONS	These findings have implications for understanding the etiology of panic disorder.
CONCLUSIONS	Previous research suggests that anxiety sensitivity is a risk factor or diathesis for this disorder, and that panic disorder is more prevalent in women than men.
CONCLUSIONS	Our findings suggest the hypothesis that the increased prevalence in women may occur because anxiety sensitivity is heritable in women.

###11252841
OBJECTIVE	It has been reported that defensiveness in women is associated with relative left-frontal electroencephalogram (EEG) activation, while defensiveness in men is associated with relative right-frontal EEG activation.
OBJECTIVE	The present study examined whether this result generalized to men and women between the ages of 58 and 70.
METHODS	The Marlowe-Crowne Social Desirability Scale (MCSD) and EEG testing were used to examine relationships between gender, defensiveness, and anterior asymmetry.
POPULATION	Men (n = 18) and women (n = 54) between the ages of 58 and 70 (mean age = 64.22, standard deviation = 3.003) were recruited from Tucson and surrounding areas of Pima County, Arizona.
POPULATION	They were selected from among participants in an integrative health sciences study of aging that took place between 1991 and 1993.
METHODS	Subjects were divided into high-defensive and low-defensive groups based on a median split on the MCSD.
METHODS	EEGs were recorded from 19 channels (standard 10-20 montage), referenced to linked ears, and digitalized on-line at 128 Hz (band pass 2-32 Hz) during consecutive 60-second eyes-open and eyes-closed baselines.
METHODS	Two-second epochs containing bioelectric artifacts > 50 microvolts were eliminated from analyses.
METHODS	Average alpha (8-13 Hz) power (microV2)) was computed by Fast Fourier Transform and natural log (ln) transformed for normalization.
METHODS	Asymmetry scores were computed (log[right]-log[left]) for F4-F3, Fp2-Fp1, F8-F7, C4-C3, T4-T3, T6-T5, P4-P3, and O2-O1 for aggregated eyes-open and eyes-closed baselines.
METHODS	R-L asymmetry scores were analyzed for the aggregate frontal lead pairs, as well as for each pair separately.
RESULTS	Findings were similar to those previously reported in that the direction of the relationship in men and women was the same.
RESULTS	The results differed in that we found left-frontal activation in low-defensive men, whereas the previous study found right-frontal activation in high-defensive men.
RESULTS	Furthermore, although in the same direction, the relationship for women was nonsignificant.
CONCLUSIONS	These differences may have been due to the age of the participants, a lax impedence criterion, or the use of the MCSD rather than the L scale of the Eysenck Personality Questionnaire, as in the earlier study.
CONCLUSIONS	Nevertheless, the findings suggest that gender differences in the relationship between resting anterior EEG asymmetry and defensiveness may persist throughout the life span.

###11252059
OBJECTIVE	To prospectively document the incidence of competition-related injury rates in an international youth soccer tournament and to analyze the type and location of injuries by age and gender.
METHODS	A prospective injury report form completed for injured players presenting to a medical facility for evaluation by the medical staff.
METHODS	An international youth soccer tournament occurring annually during mid-July.
POPULATION	89,500 soccer players, ages 9-19.
POPULATION	MEASUREMENT/MAIN RESULTS: A total of 3840 new, play-related injuries were evaluated during 290,344 player-hours of competition from 1988 through 1997.
POPULATION	New, play-related injuries per 1000-player-hours decreased from 19.87 in 1988 to 9.89 in 1997.
POPULATION	Female injury rates ranged from a maximum of 20.11 in 1989 to a minimum of 10.23 in 1996 and the male injury rate ranged from a maximum of 20.04 in 1988 to a minimum of 7.60 in 1996.
POPULATION	The lowest injury rate occurred in the under-19 females (10.64) and highest rates occurred in under-16 (17.68) and under-15 (16.92) females.
POPULATION	Heat illness correlated with mean temperature.
POPULATION	The aggregate rate of heat illness was 0.6 cases/1000 player-hours under "normal" conditions compared to a rate of 2.8/1000 player-hours during "hot" years.
CONCLUSIONS	Injury rates for both genders declined over the 10-yr span of the USA Cup study.
CONCLUSIONS	The aggregate rate of injury was slightly higher for females than males although the difference between male and female rates became less significant as the tournament matured.
CONCLUSIONS	In conditions of extreme heat and humidity (1988 and 1995) the rate of new, heat illness increased compared with normal years and females were 1.6 times more likely to sustain heat illness than males.

###11251762
OBJECTIVE	To determine how often the lists of regular medications derived in geriatric clinics by examination of "medication bags" and interview matched those found during in-home inspections and interviews.
METHODS	Prospective cross-sectional study.
METHODS	Geriatric clinics at three university-affiliated hospitals.
POPULATION	Consecutive community-dwelling outpatients 65 years or older who were newly referred.
METHODS	The in-home medication inspection used a semistructured interview followed by a room-to-room search.
METHODS	The medications identified in the home were compared with the medication list derived in clinic by examination of the "medication bag" and interview.
RESULTS	Fifty patients with a mean age of 78.9 years participated.
RESULTS	The mean number of regular medications (prescription and nonprescription) was 6.3, and the mean number of regular prescription medications was 3.6.
RESULTS	A comparison of clinic versus in-home medication lists revealed that 48% (95% confidence interval [95% CI], 34% to 62%) of patients had at least 1 omission of any regular medication and 19% (95%, CI 10% to 32%) of patients had at least 1 omission of a regular prescription medication.
CONCLUSIONS	The clinic-derived medication list resulted in a complete listing of all regular medications in only 52% taking regular medications.
CONCLUSIONS	More specific instructions to patients to bring all prescription and nonprescription medications and all vitamins, herbal, and natural remedies, and more directed questions by physicians may result in more complete clinic medication lists.

###11251749
BACKGROUND	A framework for understanding and evaluating physicians' skills at providing end of life care from the perspectives of patients, families, and health care workers will promote better quality of care at the end of life.
OBJECTIVE	To develop a comprehensive understanding of the factors contributing to the quality of physicians' care for dying patients.
METHODS	Qualitative study using focus groups and content analysis based on grounded theory.
METHODS	Seattle, Washington.
POPULATION	Eleven focus groups of patients with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, AIDS, or cancer (79 patients); 3 groups of family members who had a loved one die of chronic disease (20 family members); 4 groups of nurses and social workers from hospice or acute care settings (27 health care workers); and 2 groups of physicians with expertise in end-of-life care (11 physicians).
RESULTS	We identified 12 domains of physicians' skills at providing end-of-life care: accessibility and continuity; team coordination and communication; communication with patients; patient education; inclusion and recognition of the family; competence; pain and symptom management; emotional support, personalization; attention to patient values; respect and humility; and support of patient decision making. within these domains, we identified 55 specific components of physicians' skills.
RESULTS	Domains identified most frequently by patients and families were emotional support and communication with patients.
RESULTS	Patients with the 3 disease groups, families, and health care workers identified all 12 domains.
RESULTS	Investigators used transcript analyses to construct a conceptual model of physicians' skills at providing end-of-life care that grouped domains into 5 categories.
CONCLUSIONS	The 12 domains encompass the major aspects of physicians' skills at providing high-quality end-of-life care from the perspectives of patients, their families, and health care workers, and provide a new framework for understanding, evaluating, and teaching these skills.
CONCLUSIONS	Our findings should focus physicians, physician-educators, and researchers on communication, emotional support, and accessibility to improve the quality of end-of-life care.

###11250973
OBJECTIVE	To predict the growth in demand for long term follow up of adults with congenital heart disease.
METHODS	Observed diagnoses of congenital heart disease in infancy and childhood were adjusted for observed infant survival, predicted further survival to age 16 years, underascertainment in older childhood, and predicted need for long term follow up.
METHODS	The resident population of one health region in the UK.
POPULATION	All confirmed cardiovascular malformations diagnosed in 1985 to 1999 in children born in 1985 to 1994.
RESULTS	1942 cases of congenital heart disease were diagnosed in infancy in a population of 377 310 live births (5.2/1000). 1588 (82%) survived to 1 year and 1514 were predicted to survive to age 16. 605 further diagnoses were made in childhood-678 when adjusted for underascertainment.
RESULTS	Thus, 2192 children were predicted to reach age 16, of whom 784 would require long term follow up in adult life.
RESULTS	The adult population would comprise 28% complex, 54% significant, and 18% minor congenital heart disease.
RESULTS	These figures predict the need for adult follow up of congenital heart disease of over 200 extra cases per 100 000 live births each year or over 1600 extra cases a year every year in the UK.
CONCLUSIONS	The need for follow up of congenital heart disease in adult life is likely to grow linearly, with increasing complexity and increasing need for reinvestigation and reintervention with time.
CONCLUSIONS	Appropriate provision should be made for adequate manpower, resources, and facilities for care of these patients.

###11250970
OBJECTIVE	To describe the electrocardiographic and electrophysiological findings of new atrial flutter developing in patients taking class IC antiarrhythmic drugs for recurrent atrial fibrillation, and to report the long term results of right atrial isthmus ablation in relation to the ECG pattern of spontaneous atrial flutter.
METHODS	Retrospective analysis.
METHODS	Tertiary care academic hospital.
POPULATION	24 consecutive patients with atrial fibrillation (age 54 (12) years; 5 female, 19 male) developing atrial flutter while taking propafenone (n = 12) or flecainide (n = 12).
RESULTS	The ECG was classified as typical (n = 13; 54%) or atypical atrial flutter (n = 8) or coarse atrial fibrillation (n = 3).
RESULTS	Counterclockwise atrial flutter was the predominant arrhythmia.
RESULTS	Acute success after isthmus ablation was similar in patients with typical (12/13) and atypical (8/8) atrial flutter.
RESULTS	After long term follow up (13 (6) months, range 6-26 months), continuation of antiarrhythmic drug treatment appeared to result in better control of recurrences of atrial fibrillation in patients with typical atrial flutter (11/13) than in those with atypical atrial flutter (4/8), but the difference was not significant.
RESULTS	Ablation for coarse atrial fibrillation was unsuccessful.
CONCLUSIONS	New atrial flutter developing in patients taking class IC antiarrhythmic drugs for recurrent atrial fibrillation has either typical or atypical flutter wave morphology on ECG. The endocardial activation pattern and the acute results of ablation suggest that the flutter circuit was located in the right atrium and that the isthmus was involved in the re-entry mechanism.
CONCLUSIONS	There appeared to be better long term control of recurrent atrial fibrillation in patients with typical (85%) as compared with atypical atrial flutter (50%).
CONCLUSIONS	Patients developing coarse atrial fibrillation may not be candidates for this strategy.

###11250847
OBJECTIVE	To identify socioeconomic risk factors for first presentation advanced glaucomatous visual field loss.
METHODS	Hospital based case-control study with prospective identification of patients.
METHODS	Three hospital eye departments.
POPULATION	Consecutive patients newly diagnosed with glaucoma (n=220).
POPULATION	Cases (late presenters) were those presenting with advanced glaucoma (n=110), controls were those with early glaucoma (n=110).
RESULTS	Median underprivileged area scores were higher among late presenters (29.5; interquartile range 9.0-42.2) than in the control group (21.3; 6.1-37.4) (P=0.035).
RESULTS	Late presenters were more likely to be of lower occupational class (odds ratio adjusted for age and referral centre 20.1 (95% confidence interval 2.6 to 155) for group III compared with group I-II and 86.0 (11.0 to 673 for group IV-V compared with group I-II), to have no access to a car (2.2; 1.2 to 4.0), to have left full time education at age 14 or less (7.5; 2.3 to 24.7), and to be tenants rather than owner occupiers (local authority tenants 3.2; 1.7 to 5.8, private tenants 2.1; 0.7 to 5.8).
RESULTS	Effects of deprivation were partly accounted for by family history of glaucoma, time since last visit to an optometrist, and lack of an initial diagnosis of glaucoma by an optometrist.
CONCLUSIONS	Area and individual level deprivation were both associated with late presentation of glaucoma.
CONCLUSIONS	Existing evidence shows that late presentation is an important risk factor for subsequent blindness.
CONCLUSIONS	Deprived groups thus seem to be at greater risk of going blind from glaucoma.
CONCLUSIONS	Material deprivation may be associated with more aggressive disease as well as later presentation.

###11248716
OBJECTIVE	The purposes of this study were to describe the incidence and occurrence of femoral artery bleeding during the first 6 hours after coronary angiography and to determine whether there is a relationship between current postangiogram observation protocols and the detection of complications.
METHODS	This was a prospective descriptive study.
METHODS	The study was conducted in 3 university hospitals in Melbourne, Australia.
POPULATION	Subjects included 55 patients representing the complication rate of 1075 patients, mean age 61 years (SD, 12), 69% male.
RESULTS	About 5.1% of patients had 1 or more incidents of bleeding requiring manual compression.
RESULTS	In 4.2% of patients, bleeding occurred within 6 hours of angiography.
RESULTS	Bleeding occurred a median of 2.02 hours (Q1 = 45 minutes, Q3 = 4.31 hours) after angiography.
RESULTS	Patients without pressure bandaging bled a median of 1.32 hours (Q1 = 36.50 minutes, Q3 = 2.59 hours) after angiography.
RESULTS	Patients with pressure bandaging bled a median of 4.75 hours (Q1 = 2.25 hours, Q3 = 7.28 hours) after angiography.
RESULTS	In 40.6% of cases, bleeding was detected through the patient's call for assistance, and in 59.4% of cases nurses noted bleeding while checking the puncture site.
RESULTS	Postcatheter observations were recorded 23.70 (SD, 14.60) minutes before the bleeding incident.
RESULTS	There were no significant changes in vital signs, systolic blood pressure (P >.05), diastolic blood pressure (P >.05), or pulse (P >.05) before or during a bleeding episode.
RESULTS	All were within normal parameters.
RESULTS	No neurovascular assessment anomalies were detected.
CONCLUSIONS	The use of pressure bandaging has a significant effect on the incidence and pattern of bleeding.
CONCLUSIONS	Routine vital sign measurement has no relevance in detecting local complications after angiography.
CONCLUSIONS	The most significant complication is bleeding that requires manual compression.
CONCLUSIONS	Detection is through frequent puncture site observation and patient recognition and communication.

###11248712
OBJECTIVE	The purpose of this study was to determine the relative contribution of physical symptoms and physical functioning to depression in adult patients with heart failure during hospitalization and the early postdischarge period.
METHODS	An exploratory, correlational longitudinal design was used.
POPULATION	The sample included 170 subjects with heart failure.
RESULTS	Subjects' mean scores on the depression scale indicated that subjects were not depressed on average; however, 30% of the sample (n = 52) had scores indicative of clinical depression.
RESULTS	Both physical symptoms (r = 0.48) and physical functioning (r = -0.32) were moderately correlated with depression.
RESULTS	Physical symptoms contributed 13% uniquely to the variance in depression while physical functioning contributed only 2% uniquely to the variance in depression.
RESULTS	Multiple regression analyses indicated that physical symptomatology is more closely related to depression than is physical functioning in adults with heart failure.
CONCLUSIONS	This study showed the patients with heart failure who had increased physical symptoms and poorer physical functioning reported increased symptoms of depression.
CONCLUSIONS	Physical symptoms explained a greater portion of the variance in depression than did physical functioning.
CONCLUSIONS	Thus, it appears that patients with heart failure are affected emotionally by both their physical symptoms and their limitations in their physical functioning, but depression is more strongly related to having more physical symptoms than having greater limitations in physical functioning.

###11247887
BACKGROUND	While a close association between gastric mucosa associated lymphoid tissue (MALT) lymphoma and Helicobacter pylori infection has been established, there are still cases which do not respond to H pylori eradication.
OBJECTIVE	To investigate the clinicopathological factors which may help predict the therapeutic efficacy of H pylori eradication in gastric MALT lymphoma.
POPULATION	Forty one patients with gastric MALT lymphoma, including low and high grade lesions.
METHODS	After endosonographic staging was determined, H pylori was eradicated in all patients, and the subsequent gastric pathological course was then investigated.
RESULTS	Complete regression of MALT lymphoma was observed in 29(71%) patients, partial regression in five (12%), and no regression in seven (17%).
RESULTS	Twenty six (93%) of 28 MALT lymphomas restricted to the mucosa but only three (23%) of 13 lymphomas which invaded the deep portion of the submucosa or beyond completely regressed.
RESULTS	Kaplan-Meier analysis for the probability of complete regression of MALT lymphoma revealed a significant difference between tumours restricted to the mucosa and those invading the submucosa deeply or beyond (p<0.05).
RESULTS	Neither the presence of a high grade component, perigastric lymphadenopathy, nor clinical staging prior to eradication correlated with the probability of lymphoma regression.
CONCLUSIONS	Assessment of deep submucosal invasion by endosonography is valuable for predicting the efficacy of H pylori eradication in gastric MALT lymphoma.

###11246328
OBJECTIVE	To describe three unusual cases of sclerosing cholangitis after severe extrahepatic/extrabiliary bacterial infections.
METHODS	Case report, clinical.
METHODS	Tertiary care intensive care unit (ICU).
POPULATION	Three patients admitted to the ICU with infections from Gram-positive bacteria followed by sclerosing cholangitis and secondary biliary cirrhosis.
RESULTS	Three unusual cases of persisting cholestasis that occurred after bacterial infections originating from extrahepatic/extrabiliary foci are described.
RESULTS	Endoscopic retrograde cholangiopancreatography and magnetic resonance cholangiopancreatography revealed multiple strictures of the intrahepatic bile ducts as a sign of sclerosing cholangitis.
RESULTS	All patients progressed to biliary cirrhosis within months after the onset of cholestasis.
CONCLUSIONS	Infection-associated cholestasis is usually a functional disorder and subsides after effective treatment of the underlying inflammatory focus.
CONCLUSIONS	In rare cases, however, extrahepatic/extrabiliary infections may lead to sclerosing cholangitis and secondary biliary cirrhosis via unknown mechanisms.

###11244704
OBJECTIVE	Evaluation of the cost of propofol used for fast-track in cardiac surgery and its impact on global cost of management for anaesthesia and intensive care.
METHODS	Case-control study, prospective (1998) and retrospective (1994).
POPULATION	Twenty patients operated for cardiac surgery in 1998 and scheduled for fast-track anaesthesia.
POPULATION	Twenty patients in 1994 matched for different criteria to the patient of 1998.
METHODS	In 1998, all drugs, materials used and X-rays, biochemical assays performed were prospectively collected and their cost calculated.
METHODS	In 1994, similar calculations were done retrospectively.
METHODS	Comparison of duration of mechanical ventilation, hospitalization in intensive care and in the hospital were performed.
RESULTS	Cost of anaesthesia was similar in 1994 and 1998 (2,646 FF versus 2,294 FF).
RESULTS	Global cost of management was significantly lower in 1998 in comparison to 1994 (5,439 FF versus 8,558 FF).
RESULTS	Duration of mechanical ventilation, hospitalization in intensive care and in the hospital were shorter in 1998 than in 1994.
CONCLUSIONS	Despite a higher cost of propofol for anaesthesia and postoperative sedation in comparison to midazolam, the global cost of management decreased significantly in relation to a one day decrease in hospitalization in the intensive care unit.

###11244702
OBJECTIVE	We assessed the neuromuscular characteristics of 0.2 mg.kg-1 of mivacurium while its injection was concomitant to a non invasive blood pressure measurement in the ipsilateral arm.
POPULATION	Thirty-one patients ASA I-II were randomized into two groups.
POPULATION	Group cuff (n = 15) and Group control (n = 16).
METHODS	General anaesthesia was induced with fentanyl, thiopentone and mivacurium in all patients, however in the cuff group, measurement of non invasive blood pressure was performed immediately after the injection of mivacurium.
METHODS	Comparison was made on neuromuscular blockade of the adductor pollicis (AP) by mechanomyography, and intubating conditions which were guided by the visual estimation of the orbicularis oculi's (OO) response.
RESULTS	In the cuff group, six out of 15 patients did not have complete blockade at the OO against one out of 16 in the control group, (Fisher exact test p < 0.05).
RESULTS	Intubation time was significantly delayed in the cuff group, 201 +/- 66 s versus 123 +/- 32 s in the control group, (t test p < 0.001).
RESULTS	The maximum neuromuscular blocking effect at the AP was significantly greater in the control group 99 +/- 2% against 89 +/- 7% in the cuff group, (t test p < 0.01).
RESULTS	The onset of maximum blockade at the AP was longer in the cuff group 294 +/- 40 s versus 179 +/- 92 s, (t test p < 0.001] in the control group.
RESULTS	Time to 25% recovery was shorter in the cuff group 16 +/- 3 min versus 20 +/- 5 min, in the control group (t test p < 0.05).
CONCLUSIONS	This study suggests that non invasive blood pressure measurement of the ipsilateral arm, concomitant to the injection of mivacurium decreases the potency of mivacurium.
CONCLUSIONS	This finding is mostly explained by the early hydrolysis of mivacurium in the plasma of the excluded arm.

###11242619
OBJECTIVE	To study the minimum inhibitory concentrations (MICs) of Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) isolates to selected antibiotics from patients with acne vulgaris in Singapore and determine if resistance increases with prolonged use of antibiotics.
METHODS	A single-centre prospective study.
METHODS	Tertiary dermatological referral centre in Singapore.
POPULATION	One hundred and fifty patients with acne vulgaris seen at the National Skin Centre.
RESULTS	In patients who had never been on antibiotics, there were no resistant isolates of P. acnes.
RESULTS	In patients who had been on short-term antibiotics (between 6 to 18 weeks), there were 2 resistant strains among the 34 isolates (6.25%); in patients who had been on antibiotics for longer periods, there were 11 resistant strains among the 51 isolates (21.6%).
RESULTS	The differences in the rates of isolation of resistant strains between patients who had not been on antibiotics to those that had been on long-term antibiotics were statistically significant (P = 0.015).
RESULTS	There was also a significant difference in isolation of resistant strains from those on short-term antibiotics compared to those who had been on long-term antibiotics (P = 0.036).
RESULTS	Resistance to erythromycin was most commonly encountered.
RESULTS	Most of the erythromycin-resistant strains also showed cross-resistance to clindamycin.
RESULTS	The average MICs to antibiotics such as minocycline, erythromycin and clindamycin in those on long-term antibiotics were significantly higher when compared to patients who had not been on antibiotics.
CONCLUSIONS	Antibiotic resistance in P. acnes isolates in Singapore follows similar patterns to studies conducted in Europe.
CONCLUSIONS	Resistance to erythromycin was most commonly seen, and this is associated with cross-resistance to clindamycin.
CONCLUSIONS	Among the tetracycline group of drugs, the average MICs to tetracycline was higher than that for doxycycline, which in turn was higher than that for minocycline.
CONCLUSIONS	Antibiotic resistance can occur with short-term antibiotic courses, and the rate of resistance increases as the duration of antibiotic consumption increases.

###11241036
OBJECTIVE	Single-center studies have reported varying relapse rates after treatment of patients with acute asthma.
OBJECTIVE	We determined the relapse rate after emergency department (ED) treatment in a cohort of children.
METHODS	This was a prospective inception cohort study performed during 1997-1998.
METHODS	The study was performed in 44 EDs including both general and pediatric centers.
POPULATION	Children (n = 1184) aged 2 to 17 years who had been admitted to EDs, with acute asthma restricted to 881 patients discharged from the ED.
RESULTS	Two weeks after discharge, families were telephoned to determine relapse.
RESULTS	Follow-up data were available for 762 (86%) of the children with a 10% incidence of relapse.
RESULTS	On univariate analysis several factors were associated with relapse including current medications and markers of asthma severity.
RESULTS	On multivariate analysis the factors associated with relapse were age (OR 1.4 per 5-year increase), use of second-line asthma medications (OR 3.7), exposure to cigarette smoke (OR 0.5), and ED visits within the past year (OR 1.2 per 5 ED visits).
CONCLUSIONS	The incidence of relapse among children is lower than that observed among adults and varies with age.
CONCLUSIONS	Other risk factors such as frequent ED visits are likely markers of chronic asthma severity.
CONCLUSIONS	Further research should focus on ways to decrease the relapse rate among patients at high risk.

###11239555
OBJECTIVE	To report two cases of frozen embryo transfers in which embryos were frozen at the morula/compact stage and pregnancies were achieved after transfer.
METHODS	Case report.
METHODS	Private assisted reproductive program.
POPULATION	Two women had transfer of embryos that were frozen at the morula/compact stage.
INTERVENTION	Human morula/compact embryos were cryopreserved and transferred after subsequent thaw.
RESULTS	Embryo survival after thawing and subsequent pregnancy outcome.
RESULTS	Three and five compact embryos were frozen on day 4 for two patients, respectively.
RESULTS	In the first case, all three embryos survived after thawing, and all were transferred.
RESULTS	In the second patient, three of five embryos survived after thawing, and those three surviving embryos were transferred.
RESULTS	Pregnancies were achieved in both patients.
RESULTS	The first woman became pregnant with twins and delivered two girls weighing 2,270 g and 2,071 g, respectively.
RESULTS	The second patient became pregnant with a singleton and delivered a boy weighing 2,837 g.
CONCLUSIONS	Human embryos can be frozen and thawed in the morula/compact stage and achieve normal pregnancy.
CONCLUSIONS	Advantages of embryo freezing/thawing at the morula/compact stage include the following: [1] compared with earlier embryonic stage freezing, morula/compact-stage embryos provide better embryo selection and [2] it is easier and safer to perform assisted hatching on compact-stage embryos when compared with those from other preimplantation stages.

###11239554
OBJECTIVE	To determine whether the tyrosine autophosphorylation of the insulin receptor (IR) in the ovary of a woman with polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS) was reduced compared to normal.
METHODS	Experimental study.
METHODS	Tertiary care medical center.
POPULATION	One woman with PCOS and one healthy control, both of whom underwent a hysterectomy and bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy.
INTERVENTION	Plasma membrane fraction of ovarian tissue was isolated, and the IR was purified and concentrated.
RESULTS	Western blots of the IR, which had been incubated with and without insulin, were prepared.
RESULTS	Colorimetric and chemiluminescent methods were used to detect the presence of the IR beta-subunit and IR tyrosine autophosphorylation, respectively.
RESULTS	The presence of the beta-subunit of the IR was identified in both ovarian samples.
RESULTS	The degree of insulin-stimulated IR tyrosine autophosphorylation, reflected by the mean (+/-SD) relative optical density of the 95 kd band, was 4.3-fold higher in the normal ovary compared to the PCOS ovary (0.56 +/- 0.18 optical density vs. 0.13 +/- 0.10 optical density, respectively).
CONCLUSIONS	Tyrosine autophosphorylation of the IR may be decreased in the ovaries of women with PCOS, similar to the findings in other tissues.
CONCLUSIONS	The mechanisms through which insulin acts to produce an excess in ovarian androgen production in the face of a decrease in ovarian IR autophosphorylation remain to be determined.

###11239553
OBJECTIVE	To describe a rare case of spontaneous conception in a patient with a preexisting metastatic ovarian cancer.
METHODS	Case report.
METHODS	University hospital.
POPULATION	A 39-year-old Asian woman who conceived while undergoing an evaluation for primary infertility and newly detected bilateral adnexal masses.
INTERVENTION	Staging laparotomy and total abdominal hysterectomy and bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy.
RESULTS	Anatomic pathology diagnosis.
RESULTS	Blighted ovum and stage IIIC endometrioid adenocarcinoma of ovary.
CONCLUSIONS	Metastatic ovarian cancer does not prevent either spontaneous ovulation or spontaneous conception.

###11239552
OBJECTIVE	To report the results of a 3-year follow-up evaluation of a trial comparing goserelin acetate depot injections with sham injections before endometrial ablation for the treatment of dysfunctional uterine bleeding (DUB).
METHODS	Prospective, randomized, double-blind, parallel-group study.
METHODS	Thirty-seven centers in 12 countries.
POPULATION	Three-hundred and fifty-eight premenopausal women aged over 30 years with DUB.
INTERVENTION	Goserelin acetate (3.6 mg depot) every 28 days for 8 weeks, or sham depot every 28 days for 8 weeks, with endometrial ablation 6 weeks +/- 3 days after the first depot injection (i.e., when the endometrium is at its thinnest).
INTERVENTION	The follow-up continued for 3 years.
RESULTS	At the 3-year follow-up, bleeding in the previous 3 months and need for surgical intervention were recorded.
RESULTS	At 3 years, amenorrhea rates were 21% in the goserelin acetate group and 14% in the control group (estimated odds ratio, 1.8; 95% CI, 0.98-3.25; P=.0571).
RESULTS	The surgical intervention rate (since the original procedure) was low and did not differ significantly between groups.
RESULTS	For hysterectomy, it was 21% for the goserelin acetate group and 15% for the control group.
RESULTS	For repeat ablations, it was 5.6% for the goserelin acetate group and 2.1% for the control group.
CONCLUSIONS	Prethinning with goserelin acetate before endometrial ablation resulted in higher long-term amenorrhea rates than ablation without prethinning.

###11239551
OBJECTIVE	To describe the characteristic imaging findings of multiple globules and globules-fluid level in a cystic ovarian teratoma.
METHODS	Descriptive case study.
METHODS	Mackay Memorial Hospital and National Yang-Ming University, Taipei, Taiwan, Republic of China.
POPULATION	A 20-year-old woman with left abdominal discomfort and a palpable mass on the left side of the distended abdomen.
INTERVENTION	Surgical removal of a 10-cm cystic ovarian teratoma that contained chocolate-like fluid and multiple spheroid mobile globules consisting of yellowish sebaceous debris and fine hair shafts.
RESULTS	Plain abdominal radiography, ultrasonography, and computed tomography (CT).
RESULTS	Plain abdominal radiography revealed no calcification in the mass.
RESULTS	The first sonographic examination showed no movement by positional change of multiple rounded echogenic masses ranging from 0.5 cm to 2 cm in diameter, but the second CT examination done after the patient had spent some time lying down showed that the rounded masses arrayed in the upper portion of the cyst and displayed the characteristic level of globules-fluid.
RESULTS	Thus, the differential diagnosis was multiple mobile globules in a cystic ovarian tumor.
CONCLUSIONS	This case indicates that fatty masses need time to float upward in the fluid matrix of a cystic ovarian teratoma.
CONCLUSIONS	This delayed effect should be considered when an ovarian cyst with multiple rounded echogenic masses is diagnosed by ultrasonography, CT, or magnetic resonance imaging.

###11239550
OBJECTIVE	To compare ovarian response and IVF-ET cycle outcome in patients with hydrosalpinges managed by either laparoscopic salpingectomy or proximal tubal occlusion.
METHODS	Retrospective analysis.
METHODS	Tertiary-care assisted reproductive technology program.
POPULATION	One hundred four consecutive fresh IVF-ET cycles in 94 patients with tubal-factor infertility.
INTERVENTION	Laparoscopic salpingectomy (group 1: 35 cycles) or bipolar proximal tubal occlusion (group 2: 17 cycles), controlled ovarian hyperstimulation, and IVF-ET.
INTERVENTION	Control groups consisted of both tubal-factor patients without hydrosalpinges (group 3: 37 cycles) and those with prior bilateral tubal ligation for sterilization (group 4: 15 cycles).
RESULTS	Uterine artery Doppler flow, controlled ovarian hyperstimulation response, and implantation and clinical pregnancy rates.
RESULTS	There were no differences in mean uterine artery pulsatility indices or ovarian response among any of the groups.
RESULTS	A trend toward a higher cycle cancellation rate in group 1 did not approach statistical significance.
RESULTS	Clinical pregnancy and implantation rates were not significantly different between group 1 (57.1%, 29.2 +/- 5.9%, respectively) and group 2 (46.7%, 19.4 +/- 6.1%, respectively) or compared with those of controls.
CONCLUSIONS	[1] Management of hydrosalpinges by laparoscopic salpingectomy or bipolar proximal tubal occlusion yielded statistically similar responses to controlled ovarian hyperstimulation and IVF-ET cycle outcome.
CONCLUSIONS	[2] The latter approach may be preferable in patients who present with dense pelvic adhesions and easy access only to the proximal fallopian tube.

###11239549
OBJECTIVE	To evaluate a new minimally invasive surgery for treatment of the polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS) in clomiphene-resistant infertile women.
METHODS	Prospective study.
METHODS	University teaching hospital.
POPULATION	Thirteen clomiphene citrate-resistant anovulatory women with PCOS.
INTERVENTION	Operative transvaginal hydrolaparoscopy (THL) using a coaxial bipolar electrode.
RESULTS	Feasability, ovulation rate, and pregnancy rate.
RESULTS	Ovarian drilling was performed by THL in all patients; no surgical complications occurred.
RESULTS	Mean (+/-SD) duration of follow-up time was 6.3 +/- 3.3 months.
RESULTS	Six patients recovered to have regular ovulatory cycles.
RESULTS	Six pregnancies occurred; three were spontaneous, two occurred after stimulation and intrauterine insemination, and one occurred after IVF.
RESULTS	The cumulative pregnancy rate was 33% at 3 months after THL and 71% at 6 months after THL.
RESULTS	No miscarriages occurred.
CONCLUSIONS	Transvaginal hydrolaparoscopy with ovarian drilling using bipolar electrosurgery appears to be an alternative minimally invasive in patients with PCOS who are resistant to clomiphene therapy.

###11239548
OBJECTIVE	To present pressure lavage under ultrasound guidance (PLUG) as a new therapeutic procedure for selected cases of intrauterine adhesions (IUA).
METHODS	An open clinical investigation with no control group.
METHODS	Teaching hospital.
POPULATION	Seven consecutive patients referred to our department for secondary amenorrhea due to IUA.
INTERVENTION	A newly developed technique based on sonohysterography was used to monitor the effects of intrauterine injections of saline solution on the continuous accumulation of saline in the uterine cavity for the mechanical disruption of IUA.
RESULTS	Lysis of intrauterine adhesions, restoration of menses, and increased pregnancy rate in infertile patients.
RESULTS	Five patients with mild IUA obtained satisfactory lysis of adhesions by the use of the PLUG technique.
RESULTS	A second-look hysteroscopy after 1 month showed that filmy adhesions persisted in two patients with moderate IUA.
RESULTS	These adhesions were removed successfully during hysteroscopy.
RESULTS	Restoration of menses was obtained and has continued in all patients.
RESULTS	Two of the three infertile patients became pregnant.
CONCLUSIONS	This technique is safe and ideal as an in-office procedure.
CONCLUSIONS	PLUG allows complete lysis in mild IUA cases, and the need for therapeutic, and possibly follow-up hysteroscopy, can be avoided.
CONCLUSIONS	In moderate IUA cases, the procedure may represent a useful initial step in reducing the need for operative hysteroscopy.

###11239547
OBJECTIVE	To determine pregnancy rates after laparoscopic salpingostomy in occlusive distal tubal disease.
OBJECTIVE	To evaluate the relative impact of various historical, physical, and operative factors on pregnancy outcome using a multivariate statistical analysis.
METHODS	Prospective cohort.
METHODS	University-affiliated tertiary care infertility clinic.
POPULATION	One hundred thirty-nine infertile women with occlusive distal tube disease.
INTERVENTION	Laparoscopic salpingostomy.
RESULTS	The occurrence of intrauterine (IUP) and ectopic pregnancy (EP).
RESULTS	The overall IUP and EP rates were 24.5% and 16.5%, respectively.
RESULTS	Analysis of historical variables, assessed independently, demonstrated a significantly higher IUP rate with a positive history of gonorrhea and a significantly higher EP rate with a positive history of pelvic inflammatory disease, lack of history of intrauterine device (IUD) usage, or the performance of a bilateral procedure.
RESULTS	The logistic regression model to predict intrauterine pregnancy had an overall predictive value of 77.5% and included the following significant variables: secondary infertility, positive history of gonorrhea, and the operative finding of moderate periadnexal adhesions.
RESULTS	The logistic regression model to predict ectopic pregnancy had an overall predictive value of 89.0% and included the following significant variables: previous ectopic pregnancy, negative history of IUD use, positive history of PID, a bilateral procedure, and perihepatic adhesions.
CONCLUSIONS	Operative laparoscopy may be effective for the correction of hydrosalpinges in selected patients.
CONCLUSIONS	The probability of achieving an intrauterine or an ectopic pregnancy can be predicted based on combinations of significant variables.

###11239546
OBJECTIVE	To develop rapid and reliable techniques for determining follicle content, viability, and morphologic features in an ovarian cortical biopsy specimen.
METHODS	Prospective laboratory study.
METHODS	Academic research institution.
POPULATION	Consenting patients undergoing laparoscopic surgery for nonovarian disease.
INTERVENTION	Ovarian cortical biopsy specimens were stained by using fluorescent probes and conventional histologic stains.
INTERVENTION	Observations were made by using light, fluorescent, and confocal laser microscopy.
RESULTS	Density, cellular viability, and morphologic features of ovarian follicles.
RESULTS	After 45 minutes of incubation of ovarian cortical tissue with calcein AM, follicles were visualized by fluorescent microscopy and counted.
RESULTS	Ten minutes of exposure is sufficient to stain all cell nuclei with the PicoGreen probe.
RESULTS	Subsequent confocal microscopy allows quantification of follicle density and reveals follicle morphology in the optical sections.
RESULTS	Follicle density as assessed by using these rapid methods was concordant with the number of units counted in conventionally stained histologic sections.
CONCLUSIONS	Use of fluoroprobes for mitochondrial and DNA staining allows rapid evaluation of follicle density in ovarian tissue.

###11239545
OBJECTIVE	To determine whether seminal plasma (SP) from unexplained infertile males has different suppressive activity on antibody-dependent cellular cytotoxicity (ADCC) than SP from fertile males or SP from males of couples with known infertility factor.
METHODS	Comparative clinical/experimental study.
METHODS	In vitro fertilization program in a university hospital and a hospital research laboratory.
POPULATION	A total of 245 SP samples from 174 infertile and 16 fertile couples were compared.
INTERVENTION	SP suppression of ADCC was measured by using human 51chromium-labeled red blood cells (RBC), sensitized with IgG-rabbit-anti-human-RBC as targets and peripheral blood lymphocytes as effector cells.
RESULTS	Suppressive activity of each sample was determined by calculating 51Cr-release in the presence and absence of SP.
RESULTS	When analyzed with respect to sperm number, motility, and morphology, suppressive activities of samples with normal semen analyses (n = 142) were significantly higher (x = 37% +/- 14%) than suppressive activities of abnormal samples (n = 103; x = 32% +/- 13%).
RESULTS	There was no strong correlation of suppressive activity to other semen parameters.
RESULTS	Within the andrologically normal males, SP from the unexplained infertile couples (n = 15) showed significantly lower suppressive activity (x = 24% +/- 11%) compared with the SP from fertile males (n = 16; x = 35% +/- 13%) and from couples with female infertility factor (n = 65; x = 39% +/- 14%).
CONCLUSIONS	Loss of suppressive activity is associated with unexplained infertility, even in male patients who previously were considered normal by traditional methods of semen analysis.

###11239544
OBJECTIVE	To evaluate the cost-effectiveness analysis of hysterosalpingography (HSG), laparoscopy, and Chlamydia antibody testing (CAT) in subfertile couples.
METHODS	Cost-effectiveness analysis.
METHODS	Decision analytic framework.
POPULATION	Data of >2,000 subfertile couples in the Canadian Infertility Treatment Evaluation Study.
POPULATION	Results of CA-125 measurement and CAT were simulated from baseline characteristics.
INTERVENTION	Expectant management was considered to be the reference strategy (strategy 1).
INTERVENTION	In strategy 2 and 3, IVF was offered either immediately or after 2.5 years.
INTERVENTION	In strategy 4, the decision to offer or delay treatment was based on the couple's chance of spontaneous conception.
INTERVENTION	Nine strategies incorporated combinations of CAT, CA-125 measurement, HSG, and laparoscopy.
RESULTS	Expected live birth rates, expected number of IVF cycles, and expected total costs.
RESULTS	The strategy starting with CAT was the most cost-effective in couples whose 3-year chance of conception was >14%, whereas the strategy starting with HSG was the most cost-effective in couples with worse fertility prospects.
CONCLUSIONS	The diagnostic work-up to detect tubal pathology in subfertile couples should start with CAT in couples with relatively good fertility prospects and immediate HSG in couples with relatively poor fertility prospects.

###11239543
OBJECTIVE	To explore a possible association between estrogen receptor-alpha (ER-alpha) gene polymorphisms and development of uterine leiomyomas.
METHODS	Case-control study.
METHODS	University teaching hospital.
POPULATION	119 women with clinically and surgically diagnosed uterine leiomyomas.
INTERVENTION	Therapeutic hysterectomy.
RESULTS	Frequency and distribution of ER-alpha gene polymorphisms.
RESULTS	No statistically significant differences between controls and patients in the allele frequency and genotype distribution were found when Pvu II and Xba I restriction polymorphism sites were analyzed separately.
RESULTS	When the two ER-alpha gene polymorphisms were analyzed in combination, five major genotypes were recognized in controls or patients; the frequency differed slightly but not significantly between groups.
CONCLUSIONS	The Pvu II and Xba I polymorphisms in the ER-alpha gene do not produce different risks of developing uterine leiomyomas.

###11239542
OBJECTIVE	To evaluate the reproductive outcome after ectopic pregnancy and to assess the contribution of risk factors to future fertility.
METHODS	Prospective follow-up in a population-based sample.
METHODS	Register of ectopic pregnancies established in an urban area around Lille, France.
POPULATION	Three hundred and twenty-eight women treated between April 1994 and March 1997 who had not been using an IUCD at the time of the ectopic pregnancy and were trying to become pregnant.
INTERVENTION	Interviews by telephone every 6 months for 2 years and once yearly thereafter.
RESULTS	Cumulative pregnancy rate.
RESULTS	Two hundred fifteen (65.5%) women became pregnant after a mean of 5 months.
RESULTS	One hundred eighty-two (84.7%) pregnancies were intrauterine; 22 (10.2%) were recurrent ectopic pregnancies; and in 11 women (5.1%), it was too early to define implantation.
RESULTS	The cumulative intrauterine pregnancy rate was 56% at 1 year and 67% at 2 years.
RESULTS	After applying Cox regression, three factors associated with fertility seemed to decrease reproductive performance: age > 35 years, history of infertility, and anterior tubal damage .
CONCLUSIONS	More than half of the women treated for ectopic pregnancy spontaneously conceived and had a normally progressive pregnancy at 1 year.
CONCLUSIONS	Fertility depends more on established patient characteristics than characteristics of ectopic pregnancy itself or treatment thereof.

###11239541
OBJECTIVE	To determine serum parameters reflective of androgen status in postmenopausal women using two types of hormone replacement therapy (HRT).
METHODS	Randomized, double-blind, prospective 1-year trial of two oral HRT regimens.
METHODS	University hospital, department of obstetrics and gynecology, menopause clinic.
POPULATION	100 postmenopausal women > or = 45 years.
INTERVENTION	Daily use of the progestogen tibolone (2.5 mg; n = 50) or continuous combined 17-beta-estradiol (2 mg) and norethindrone acetate (E+NA, 1 mg; n = 50).
RESULTS	Measurements of total testosterone (total T), dehydroepiandrosterone sulfate (DHEAS), androstenedione (A), FSH, and sex-hormone-binding globulin (SHBG), and calculations of free testosterone (free T).
RESULTS	Assessment of changes from baseline within and between groups after 6 and 12 months.
RESULTS	We found significant differences (% changes) in the tibolone group compared to baseline within the groups after both 6 and 12 months, respectively.
RESULTS	Levels of free T doubled, total T decreased slightly, and SHBG decreased by half; DHEAS increased by approximately 20%; and FSH decreased.
RESULTS	In the E+NA group, levels of free T, total T, androstenedione, and FSH all decreased, and SHBG increased.
RESULTS	Pre-trial levels of DHEAS, A, and total T were significantly higher in the E+NA group.
RESULTS	Between groups throughout the study, the changes from baseline were significant due to the different extent of FSH reduction, and opposite changes of free T, SHBG, and DHEAS.
CONCLUSIONS	Both regimens modify plasma androgens, DHEAS, and SHBG differently.
CONCLUSIONS	Tibolone decreased the levels of SHBG, and substantially increased free T and to a lesser extent increased DHEAS; this may reflect a modification of adrenal androgen production.
CONCLUSIONS	Continuous combined estradiol and norethindrone acetate HRT suppressed the peripheral plasma androgens mediated by increased levels of SHBG.

###11239540
OBJECTIVE	To determine the hormonal characteristics of a fecund menstrual cycle.
METHODS	Prospective observational study.
METHODS	Clinical research center.
POPULATION	Fourteen patients having artificial insemination with donor semen provided daily blood and urine samples in a nonconceptive cycle and the consecutive conceptive cycle.
INTERVENTION	Concentrations of E2, luteinizing hormone, follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH), and P4 were measured in serum.
INTERVENTION	Urine samples were analyzed for metabolites of E2, P4, and FSH.
RESULTS	The serum and urinary hormone profiles of the periovulatory period were compared in conceptive and nonconceptive cycles.
RESULTS	The mean peak value of periovulatory urinary FSH was significantly higher in conceptive cycles than in nonconceptive cycles.
RESULTS	The mean serum E2 concentration was significantly higher on day 0 (day of peak urinary FSH concentration) in conceptive cycles than in nonconceptive cycles, but mean peak values of serum E2 did not differ significantly.
RESULTS	No other significant differences were observed in serum and urinary hormone profiles between conceptive and nonconceptive cycles.
CONCLUSIONS	A lower, broader peak of FSH in urine was observed in nonconceptive cycles compared with conceptive cycles.
CONCLUSIONS	Urinary FSH measurements may be useful in predicting less fecund ovulatory cycles and may identify some types of reduced female fertility.

###11239539
OBJECTIVE	To examine the thoughts and concerns of men contemplating vasectomy before speaking to a physician as well as their partner's role in reaching this decision.
METHODS	A questionnaire analysis using response rates, ANOVA, and regression analyses.
METHODS	A large Midwestern teaching hospital.
POPULATION	Visitors to the urology clinic of the hospital.
INTERVENTION	None.
RESULTS	[1] Questionnaire designed by authors.
RESULTS	[2] Measures of self-concept, relationship satisfaction, and problem-solving abilities.
RESULTS	[1] Subjects had been considering vasectomy for an average of 1 year and were fairly certain of their decision.
RESULTS	[2] Anxiety about vasectomy surgery was mostly driven by fear about pain and fear of the unknown.
RESULTS	[3] Concerns about the finality of the procedure did not emerge as a big concern.
RESULTS	[4] There is confusion about the reversibility of the procedure.
RESULTS	[5] Subjects are better problem solvers and have a higher self-concept than people in general.
CONCLUSIONS	Our findings demonstrate the need for adequate prevasectomy counseling, particularly in the area of postoperative expectations, as well as reversibility of the procedure.

###11239538
OBJECTIVE	To predict the risk of premature ovulation and possibility of pregnancy in natural IVF/ICSI cycles on the basis of the estradiol (E2) level on the day of hCG injection and on the day after.
METHODS	A prospective study.
METHODS	Hospital research program.
POPULATION	One hundred sixty-four women undergoing 305 IVF/ICSI natural cycles.
INTERVENTION	Serum E2 levels were measured approximately 12 h before (day 0) and 12-17 h after hCG administration (day 1).
RESULTS	E2 levels on day 0 and day 1, the ratio of the day 1 to day 0 levels, and cycle outcome.
RESULTS	In cycles with premature ovulation and in conception cycles, the average E2 level on day 0 was statistically significantly higher than in other cycles, whereas the E2 ratio was statistically significantly lower.
RESULTS	Multiple logistic regression was used to determine the connection of the E2 level on day 0 (B0 = -0.742, B = 2.147, P =.01) and the E2 ratio (B0 = -0.742, B = -3.135, P<.001) with premature ovulation.
RESULTS	Only the E2 ratio (B0 = 0.659, B = -2.209, P =.0068) was significantly connected with conception.
CONCLUSIONS	In predicting the outcome of natural IVF/ICSI cycles, the importance lies not in the E2 level on the day of hCG administration or on the day after, but rather in the E2 ratio.

###11239537
OBJECTIVE	To determine the permeability of unfertilized human oocytes to water and the cryoprotectant propane-1,2-diol over a range of temperatures and to use these data to predict osmotic responses under given conditions.
METHODS	Laboratory-based study.
METHODS	Teaching hospital.
POPULATION	Infertility patients donating unfertilized oocytes in excess of those required for treatment.
INTERVENTION	None.
RESULTS	Water and cryoprotectant permeability were determined from measurements of oocyte volume excursions on exposure to 1.5 M propane-1,2-diol at 30 degrees C, 24 degrees C, and 10 degrees C.
RESULTS	Permeability of human oocytes to water and cryoprotectant increased as temperature increased.
RESULTS	The predicted response of oocytes, based on these data, closely matched the measured response of an oocyte on exposure to a widely used method for addition of cryoprotectant before freezing.
CONCLUSIONS	Commonly used cryopreservation protocols involving slow cooling in the presence of propane-1,2-diol cause potentially damaging excursions in cell volume on exposure to cryoprotectant.
CONCLUSIONS	Modifications that can be expected to reduce cell volume excursions, based on oocyte permeability data, are suggested.

###11239536
OBJECTIVE	To compare the value of 17 IVF variables, including a new mean score of transferred embryos (MSTE), in predicting pregnancy rate.
METHODS	Retrospective study.
METHODS	Private IVF unit.
POPULATION	Women who underwent 10,000 embryo transfers.
RESULTS	Duration of infertility, type of infertility, female age, rank of IVF attempt, type of ovarian treatment, progesterone level, sperm count, sperm motility, sperm morphology, number of retrieved oocytes, number of mature oocytes, maturation rate, number of embryos obtained, fertilization rate, number of transferred embryos, cumulative embryo score (CES), mean score of transferred embryos (MSTE), and pregnancy rate.
RESULTS	Outcome of IVF-ET was significantly correlated with female age, type of infertility, number of retrieved oocytes, number of mature oocytes, maturation rate, embryos obtained, fertilization rate, transferred embryos, CES, and MSTE.
RESULTS	Multivariate analysis demonstrated that MSTE was a better predictor of pregnancy than the number of transferred embryos and female age.
CONCLUSIONS	Embryo quality is the best predictor of pregnancy.
CONCLUSIONS	The embryo score described herein should be used in IVF-ET programs to choose the best embryos for transfer.

###11239535
OBJECTIVE	To study the effect of endogenous luteinizing hormone (LH) concentration on fertilization, pregnancy, and early pregnancy loss rates.
METHODS	Retrospective cohort study.
METHODS	Tertiary-care university center.
POPULATION	One hundred sixty-six normogonadotropic patients undergoing IVF.
INTERVENTION	Luteal phase pituitary down-regulation and recombinant FSH (Gonal-F) were used for ovarian stimulation.
INTERVENTION	The mean of 4-5 serum LH concentrations, from stimulation days 5-12, was computed for analysis.
RESULTS	Fertilization, pregnancy, and early pregnancy loss rates according to periovulatory levels of LH.
RESULTS	Data were analyzed by stratifying patients according to a mean periovulatory LH value of 3 mIU/mL.
RESULTS	After controlling for confounding variables with logistic regression, results showed that the fertilization rate was significantly lower in patients with a periovulatory LH <3 mIU/mL versus > or = 3 mIU/mL (52% and 58%, respectively; P=.03).
RESULTS	Pregnancy rates and spontaneous abortion rates were similar in both groups.
RESULTS	There were seven biochemical pregnancies, all in patients with an LH <3 mIU/mL (P=.07).
CONCLUSIONS	Low endogenous LH concentrations (<3 mIU/mL) in the late follicular phase of an IVF cycle are associated with significantly lower fertilization rates and a trend toward higher biochemical pregnancy rates.
CONCLUSIONS	It may be of clinical benefit, when exclusively using r-hFSH in ART cycles, to add LH in the late follicular phase or to further reduce the dose of GnRH agonist.

###11239534
OBJECTIVE	To determine the feasibility and success of blastocyst-stage embryo transfers in patients having only fair and poor quality cleavage-stage embryos on day 3.
METHODS	Prospective case study with historic controls.
METHODS	Tertiary care private hospital IVF center.
POPULATION	A total of 158 day 5 embryo transfer cycles in patients with grade 3 and grade 4 cleavage-stage embryos.
POPULATION	Control group consisted of 162 day 3 transfer cycles performed with embryos of similar quality.
INTERVENTION	In vitro culture of embryos up to the blastocyst stage.
RESULTS	The percentage of cycles that culminated in the transfer of at least one blastocyst and implantation and pregnancy rate related to the day of transfer.
RESULTS	In the day 3 transfer group, a mean of 5.2 embryos were replaced per patient.
RESULTS	This was significantly more than the mean of 2.4 embryos that could be replaced on day 5 (P <.001).
RESULTS	The clinical pregnancy rate per embryo transfer was 27.2% and 33.5% in the two groups, respectively (P >.05).
RESULTS	The implantation rate per embryo was significantly higher in the day 5 transfer group (15% vs. 5.9%).
RESULTS	The multiple pregnancy and abortion rates were similar between the groups.
CONCLUSIONS	Transfer of fair and poor quality embryos at the blastocyst stage is feasible and is associated with higher implantation rates as compared to transfer of similar quality embryos on day 3.

###11239533
OBJECTIVE	To compare implantation and pregnancy rates in oocyte recipients undergoing a two-embryo versus three-embryo transfer, 3 days after retrieval.
METHODS	Retrospective comparative analysis.
METHODS	University-based in vitro fertilization center.
POPULATION	All oocyte recipients undergoing embryo transfer from January 1, 1997 through August 31, 1999.
INTERVENTION	Recipients received two or three embryos.
RESULTS	Implantation, and clinical and multiple pregnancy rates.
RESULTS	Seventy-three recipients underwent a two-embryo transfer, and 376 had three embryos replaced.
RESULTS	The numbers of oocytes retrieved (12.7 +/- 0.89 vs. 13.1 +/- 0.36) and embryos obtained (8.05 +/- 0.65 vs. 8.77 +/- 0.27) did not differ between the two-embryo and three-embryo transfer groups, nor did the proportion of patients with embryo cryopreservation (54.3% vs. 42.6%, respectively).
RESULTS	There was no significant difference in pregnancy or implantation rates when comparing those patients with a two-embryo transfer to those with a three-embryo transfer.
RESULTS	Significantly, 13.8% of the pregnancies in the three-embryo transfer group were triplet.
CONCLUSIONS	Reducing the number of embryos transferred in an oocyte donation cycle can lower the incidence of triplet pregnancies without significantly lowering the overall pregnancy rate.

###11239532
OBJECTIVE	To determine if metformin therapy improves in vitro fertilization (IVF) outcomes in patients with clomiphene-resistant polycystic ovarian syndrome (PCOS).
METHODS	Retrospective data analysis of selective groups of patients.
METHODS	A private IVF unit.
POPULATION	Forty-six women with clomiphene citrate-resistant PCOS underwent 60 cycles of IVF embryo transfer with intracytoplasmic sperm injection.
INTERVENTION	In half of the cycles, patients received metformin (1000 to 1500 mg) daily, starting the cycle prior to gonadotropin treatment.
RESULTS	Total number of follicles; serum estradiol (E2) on the day of hCG administration and the cycle's E2 maximum; total number of oocytes, mature oocytes, embryos, fertilization, and pregnancy rates; and follicular fluid levels of insulin-like growth factors (IGF-I, IGF-II) and IGF-binding proteins (IGFBP-1, IGFBP-3).
RESULTS	In patients treated with metformin, the total number of follicles on the day of hCG treatment was decreased (23 +/- 1.2 vs. 33 +/- 2.6) with no change in follicles > or = 14 mm in diameter (21 +/- 1.2 vs. 25 +/- 1.7).
RESULTS	Metformin treatment did not affect the mean number of oocytes retrieved (22 +/- 1.9 vs. 20.3 +/- 1.5).
RESULTS	However, the mean number of mature oocytes (18.4 +/- 1.5 vs. 13 +/- 1.5) and embryos cleaved (12.5 +/- 1.5 vs. 5.9 +/- 0.9) were increased after metformin treatment.
RESULTS	Fertilization rates (64% vs. 43%) and clinical pregnancy rates (70% vs.30%) were also increased.
RESULTS	Metformin led to modulation of preovulatory of follicular fluid IGF levels with increases of IGF-I (140 +/- 8 vs. 109 +/- 7ng/mL) and decreased of IGFBP-1 (133 +/- 8 vs.153 +/- 9ng/mL).
CONCLUSIONS	Metformin use appears to improve IVF outcomes in patients with clomiphene citrate-resistant PCOS.

###11239531
OBJECTIVE	To evaluate adrenal steroid responses to ACTH stimulation and insulin responses to oral glucose tolerance test (OGTT) in clomiphene-citrate-resistant anovulatory women with polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS) before and after laparoscopic ovarian drilling.
METHODS	Prospective study.
METHODS	Healthy participants in a university teaching hospital.
POPULATION	Twenty clomiphene-citrate-resistant anovulatory women with PCOS.
INTERVENTION	Laparoscopic ovarian drilling.
RESULTS	Adrenal steroid responses to ACTH stimulation and insulin responses to OGTT before and after ovarian drilling.
RESULTS	Fourteen of 20 women completed the study.
RESULTS	There was a correlation between body mass index and basal insulin levels before ovarian drilling.
RESULTS	This correlation was lost after surgery.
RESULTS	Area-under-the-curve glucose and insulin values before surgery in women with hyperinsulinemia (1,033.3 +/- 112.7 mmol/L and 141,919 +/- 26,177 pmol/L, respectively) were significantly higher than in those with normoinsulinemia (777.1 +/- 77.2 mmol/L and 69,867 +/- 19,390 pmol/L respectively, P<.05).
RESULTS	There was a significant difference in insulin and glucose responses before and after ovarian drilling in women with hyperinsulinemia.
RESULTS	No difference was found in women with normoinsulinemia.
RESULTS	There was no significant difference in adrenal steroid responses to ACTH stimulation before and after ovarian drilling.
CONCLUSIONS	PCOS women with hyperinsulinemia respond differently to laparoscopic ovarian drilling than do those with normoinsulinemia.
CONCLUSIONS	Among women with hyperinsulinemia, surgery decreases glucose and insulin responses to OGTT.
CONCLUSIONS	Regardless of the insulin level, laparoscopic ovarian drilling does not influence adrenal steroid dynamics.

###11239530
