OBJECTIVE	To compare the clinical efficacy and safety of the combination of Diane 35 (2 mg of cyproterone acetate, and 35 microg of ethinyl estradiol) plus finasteride (5 mg), and Diane 35 alone in the treatment of hirsutism.
METHODS	Prospective randomized clinical study.
METHODS	Outpatients in Erciyes University Medical School.
POPULATION	Forty women with hirsutism were selected.
INTERVENTION	For 1 year, group 1 patients (n = 20) were treated with Diane 35 alone (2 mg of cyproterone acetate and 35 microg of ethinyl estradiol) daily on days 5 to 25 of the menstrual cycle and group 2 patients (n = 20) with Diane 35 plus finasteride (5 mg daily).
RESULTS	Hirsutism was graded at 6-month intervals using the Ferriman-Gallwey method.
RESULTS	The basal hormone levels of total and free testosterone (T), androstenedione, DHEAS, and sex-hormone-binding globulin (SHBG) were measured by radioimmunoassay before the study.
RESULTS	Total T, free T, SHBG, and DHEAS were also measured at 6-month intervals for 1 year.
RESULTS	Multiscreen blood chemistry and side effects were evaluated during the treatment.
RESULTS	Thirty-four patients completed the 12-month study period.
RESULTS	A significant decrease in the hirsutism score as compared to baseline was observed after 12 months with both Diane 35 treatment (mean +/- SD, 15.62 +/- 4.89 vs. 9.75 +/- 3.97) and Diane 35 plus finasteride treatment (16.27 +/- 6.90 vs. 8.38 +/- 4.44).
RESULTS	The percentage decreases in the hirsutism score (mean percent +/- SD) were 30.26 +/- 14.56 vs. 34.70 +/- 11.60 at 6 months, 38.09 +/- 11.46 vs. 48.14 +/- 14.27 at 12 months in the Diane 35 and the Diane 35 plus finasteride groups, respectively.
RESULTS	The percentage reduction in the hirsutism score in the Diane 35 plus finasteride group at 12 months was greater than in the Diane 35 group (P <.05).
CONCLUSIONS	The percentage decrease in the hirsutism score at 12 months was higher in the Diane 35 plus finasteride group than in the Diane 35 group.
CONCLUSIONS	We believe that Diane 35 plus finasteride is an effective and safe combination for the treatment of hirsutism.

###11239529
OBJECTIVE	To compare the levels of IL-1beta, IL-6, and TNFalpha in endometriotic tissue and in endometrium from women with endometriosis and healthy controls.
METHODS	Open.
METHODS	Department of Obstetrics and Gynecology at a university hospital.
POPULATION	Twenty-six women with endometriosis and 22 controls operated on for clinical indications.
INTERVENTION	ELISA in homogenized tissue samples collected during surgery.
RESULTS	Levels of IL-1beta, IL-6, and TNFalpha in tissue homogenates.
RESULTS	The three types of tissue differed significantly with respect to all three cytokines.
RESULTS	Endometriotic tissue had significantly higher concentrations of IL-1beta than endometrium from both patients with endometriosis and healthy controls.
RESULTS	Both endometriotic tissue and endometrium from patients had significantly higher concentrations of IL-6, and endometriotic tissue had significantly lower concentration of TNFalpha than did endometrium from controls.
RESULTS	IL-1beta showed a cycle phase dependence that was significant in endometrium from patients, being higher in the secretory than in the follicular phase.
RESULTS	IL-1beta was significantly higher in endometrioma than in lesions of other localizations.
RESULTS	Concentrations of IL-1beta and IL-6 were positively correlated in endometriotic tissue and in endometrium from controls.
RESULTS	No other significant correlations were found.
CONCLUSIONS	This study has shown a significant production of IL-1beta, IL-6, and TNFalpha in endometriotic tissue and endometrium, with significant differences between the tissue types, indicating a deviating cytokine pattern in both endometriotic tissue and endometrium from women with endometriosis compared with that from healthy controls.

###11239528
OBJECTIVE	To evaluate the effectiveness of a levonorgestrel-releasing IUD as therapy for endometriosis of the rectovaginal septum.
METHODS	Prospective therapeutic non-randomized, self-controlled clinical trial analyzing changes in pain symptoms and size of lesions induced by the levonorgestrel-releasing IUD over 12 months.
METHODS	Tertiary referral center for treatment of deep endometriosis.
POPULATION	Eleven symptomatic patients with rectovaginal endometriosis.
INTERVENTION	A levonorgestrel-releasing IUD was inserted and maintained for 12 months.
RESULTS	Severity of dysmenorrhea, pelvic pain, and deep dyspareunia were assessed before insertion of the IUD and throughout treatment.
RESULTS	The size of rectovaginal endometriotic lesions were evaluated by using transrectal and transvaginal ultrasonography.
RESULTS	Dysmenorrhea, pelvic pain, and deep dyspareunia greatly improved and the size of the endometriotic lesions was significantly reduced by treatment.
CONCLUSIONS	Insertion of a levonorgestrel-releasing IUD alleviates pain and reduces the size of lesions in patients with endometriosis of the rectovaginal septum.

###11239527
OBJECTIVE	Hypo-osmotic swelling test (HOST) has been shown to be an effective method for the selection of live sperm.
OBJECTIVE	On-going pregnancies were obtained by using HOST-selected sperm.
OBJECTIVE	The aim of this study was to evaluate the effect of using HOST-selected "live" sperm versus nonselected sperm on the outcome of intracytoplasmic sperm injection cycles when only nonmotile sperm were available for injection.
METHODS	Prospective randomized study.
METHODS	Governmental tertiary care hospital.
POPULATION	Thirty ICSI cycles with no motile sperm were included in this study.
INTERVENTION	For the HOST group, potentially live spermatozoa detected by hypo-osmotic reaction of the tail were injected into oocytes.
INTERVENTION	For the No-HOST group, the sperm were randomly injected into the oocytes without checking the viability.
RESULTS	The fertilization, cleavage, embryo quality, pregnancy, and implantation rates were assessed for the two groups.
RESULTS	Among 30 cycles, 15 fall into each group.
RESULTS	Fertilization, cleavage rates, and the number of good quality embryos were similar between two groups.
CONCLUSIONS	HOST-selected live spermatozoa can be safely used for intracytoplasmic sperm injection to establish pregnancies.
CONCLUSIONS	There is a tendency for higher pregnancy and implantation rates to result, but it does not reach statistical significance.

###11239526
OBJECTIVE	To determine the presence or absence of the deleted in azoospermia (DAZ) gene clusters in the Y-bearing spermatozoa in semen of severely oligozoospermic men or in testicular biopsy samples of azoospermic men with somatic DAZ deletions.
METHODS	Prospective study.
METHODS	Academic hospital.
POPULATION	Nineteen patients attending our clinics for therapeutic intracytoplasmic injection of sperm.
INTERVENTION	Peripheral blood lymphocytes were used to obtain somatic DNA for analysis using the polymerase chain reaction.
INTERVENTION	Analysis of chromosomes X and Y and the detection of the DAZ gene clusters were carried out with the fluorescence in situ hybridization technique in spermatozoa remaining after intracytoplasmic sperm injection.
RESULTS	Presence or absence of the DAZ gene clusters in matched somatic DNA and Y-bearing spermatozoa.
RESULTS	Seven patients appeared to have a somatic DAZ deletion.
RESULTS	Three-color fluorescence in situ hybridization showed that all Y-bearing spermatozoa examined from these men carried the same deletion.
CONCLUSIONS	The DAZ deletions present in the seven men would all have been transmitted if they had fathered sons through artificial fertilization techniques using the sperm cells examined in this study.

###11239525
OBJECTIVE	To examine the relationship between sperm chromatin defects, evaluated by sperm chromatin structure assay (SCSA) and semen characteristics in cryopreserved semen specimens from patients diagnosed with various types of cancer.
METHODS	Prospective study.
METHODS	Andrology laboratory at a tertiary care hospital.
POPULATION	Cryopreserved semen samples from 12 healthy fertile men and 37 men diagnosed with cancer: testicular cancer (n = 20), Hodgkin's disease (n = 11), non-Hodgkin's disease (n = 4), and other neoplasm (n = 2).
INTERVENTION	None.
RESULTS	The shift of green (native DNA) to red (denatured, single-stranded DNA) fluorescence in acridine orange-stained nuclei was measured and quantified using the expression alpha(t)(red fluorescence/[red + green fluorescence] per cell).
RESULTS	Sperm DNA damage was correlated with classical semen characteristics.
RESULTS	Cancer patients as a group had significantly higher DNA damage when compared with controls.
RESULTS	Specimens with high COMPalpha(t) values (percentage of sperm with denatured DNA) were present in all groups of cancer patients.
RESULTS	No meaningful correlation was seen between the extent of DNA damage and classical semen measures.
CONCLUSIONS	DNA damage in spermatozoa is prevalent in the majority of cancer patients.
CONCLUSIONS	SCSA provides important information about the biochemical integrity of sperm DNA in men with cancer before their treatment.

###11239323
OBJECTIVE	To assess the efficacy of focal intense brief transcutaneous electric nerve stimulation in 2 groups of patients with neuropathic pain.
METHODS	Electric stimulation was delivered with a small stylus electrode pressed against the skin paravertebrally in the radiculopathy group and along the surgical incision in the postthoracotomy neuralgia group.

POPULATION	Fourteen consecutive patients with well-documented radiculopathy and 4 consecutive patients with postthoracotomy neuralgia.
RESULTS	Statistically significant differences were found in both patient groups, with posttreatment scores reflecting less pain than pretreatment scores for every patient.
CONCLUSIONS	Patients reported reduced pain ratings following this stimulation technique.
CONCLUSIONS	Further study of this inexpensive, noninvasive, outpatient technique is needed.

###11239314
OBJECTIVE	To evaluate the validity and reliability of 2 measurement devices that assess pelvic mobility in persons bending forward while in a standing position.
METHODS	Validity and reliability studies.
POPULATION	The validity study included 10 patients (4 men, 6 women) aged 42 (range, 33--51yr).
POPULATION	The reliability study included 50 subjects (25 men, 25 women) aged 30 (range, 18--49yr).
INTERVENTION	A 3-dimensional ultrasound motion analyzer (CMS 50) and a spine motion analyzer using potentiometers (Rachimètre).
METHODS	Two lateral radiographs of the patients' lumbosacral junction were taken, the first in neutral position, the second in full trunk flexion.
METHODS	Correlations between mobilities assessed by radiographs and both devices were evaluated by Spearman's rank correlation coefficient.
METHODS	Reliability was studied in healthy volunteers using the intraclass coefficient correlation (ICC) and the Bland and Altman plot.
RESULTS	Spearman's coefficient between radiographic measures and the Rachimètre and the CMS 50 evaluations were.89 and.81, respectively.
RESULTS	For the Rachimètre, ICC was.65 and increased with a better standardization of the measurement procedures.
RESULTS	For the CMS 50, ICC was.85, and the Bland and Altman plot revealed no systematic trend.
CONCLUSIONS	The Rachimètre and the CMS 50 have acceptable metric properties.
CONCLUSIONS	Because few simple clinical measurements are available to evaluate pelvic mobility during trunk flexion in standing position, these 2 devices could be useful in the clinical evaluation of low back pain.

###11239312
OBJECTIVE	To examine the homogeneity, test-retest reliability, construct validity, and concurrent validity of the Impact on Participation and Autonomy Questionnaire (IPAQ).
METHODS	Cross-sectional study with a test-retest subsample.
POPULATION	One hundred twenty-six persons from 5 diagnostic groups recruited from the outpatients clinics of 2 rehabilitation centers and the rehabilitation department of an academic hospital.
INTERVENTION	The IPAQ and 3 other self-administered questionnaires (Sickness Impact Profile [68-item version], London Handicap Scale [LHS], Medical Outcome Study Short-Form Health Survey).
INTERVENTION	The IPAQ was completed twice by 75 respondents within approximately 2 weeks.
RESULTS	The IPAQ addresses autonomy and participation in 5 domains: autonomy indoors, family role, autonomy outdoors, social relations, and work and educational opportunities.
RESULTS	Cronbach's alpha for the several domains ranged between.81 and.91, indicating good homogeneity.
RESULTS	On item level, weighted kappas ranged between.56 and.90.
RESULTS	On domain level, the test-retest reliability of the IPAQ was good: intraclass correlation coefficients ranged between.83 and.91.
RESULTS	Convergent validity was largely supported by the correlations between 4 domains of the LHS and the IPAQ.
RESULTS	Discriminant validity was best demonstrated by low correlations between the IPAQ and 2 domains of the LHS representing theoretically different constructs.
CONCLUSIONS	The IPAQ is a reliable and valid instrument for assessing autonomy and participation in chronic disorders.
CONCLUSIONS	Its responsiveness requires further study.

###11239289
OBJECTIVE	To assess normalization in the lives of liver transplant patients and the impact of preoperative expectations on postoperative quality of life (QOL).
METHODS	A semistructured interview, 2 QOL questionnaires, and chart reviews of medical histories.
METHODS	Internal medicine department at Innsbruck university hospital, Austria.
POPULATION	Fifty-five patients (32 men, 23 women) with liver transplants.
INTERVENTION	The Sickness Impact Profile (SIP) and Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy-General.
RESULTS	Patients' preoperative expectations of a normal life style posttransplantation were predominantly optimistic (60%), but postoperatively only 40% thought that their expectations had been realized.
RESULTS	The patients' SIP values showed significant impairments in nearly every area of life when compared with the values of a healthy control group.
RESULTS	Only "complications during the hospitalized phase" had a statistically significant impact among the sociodemographic and clinical parameters on postoperative QOL.
RESULTS	The lowest QOL scores were found among patients whose expectations of a return to normal life style had not been realized.
CONCLUSIONS	Unmet life-style expectations after liver transplantation may lead to increased stress, which affects QOL long term.
CONCLUSIONS	This finding is of clinical relevance; therapeutic measures, particularly professional pretransplant counseling, are indicated.

###11239140
OBJECTIVE	To describe the relationship between patient satisfaction with out of hours care provided by deputising and practice doctors in four urban areas in England and characteristics of the service provided and patients, the care given, and health outcomes.
METHODS	-Fourteen general practices in four urban areas in England.
POPULATION	People who requested out of hours care.
METHODS	Analysis of data from a study of out of hours care.
METHODS	Patients were interviewed within 5 days of their request for out of hours care.
METHODS	Data on the service provided were obtained from medical records and all other data were collected at interview.
METHODS	Satisfaction was measured using a valid reliable instrument.
RESULTS	2152 patients were recruited to the study and 1466 were interviewed.
RESULTS	Satisfaction data were available on 1402 patients.
RESULTS	"Overall satisfaction" was associated with age, doctor type, lack of access to a car at the time of the request, and health outcome.
RESULTS	The relationships between satisfaction subscales and patient characteristics (age, sex, ethnicity, and access to a car at the time of the request), service characteristics (doctor type and delay between the request and visit), whether a prescription was given, and health outcome were variable.
RESULTS	If an expected home visit was not received, "overall satisfaction" and satisfaction with "communication and management", "doctor's attitude", and "initial contact person" were reduced.
CONCLUSIONS	Patient satisfaction is dependent on many factors.
CONCLUSIONS	Mismatch between patient expectation and the service received is related to decreased satisfaction.
CONCLUSIONS	This may increase as general practitioners delegate more out of hours care to cooperatives and deputising services.

###11236116
OBJECTIVE	To investigate the effect of glyceryl trinitrate on isolated human pregnant uterine strips and whether the uterine relaxation induced by glyceryl trinitrate could be reversed by oxytocics used in current clinical practice.
METHODS	In vitro pharmacological study.
METHODS	Department of Obstetrics & Gynaecology, National University of Singapore, National University Hospital.
POPULATION	Eighteen women who delivered by caesarean section at term.
METHODS	Myometrial strips were preloaded with an initial tension of 1.5g in organ baths containing Krebs-Henseleit solution which was aerated with oxygen in 5% carbon dioxide and maintained at 37 degrees C, pH 7.4.
METHODS	The effect of glyceryl trinitrate was studied in strips displaying regular spontaneous contractions.
METHODS	The ability of oxytocin, ergometrine or prostaglandin F2alpha to stimulate uterine contractions was assessed in strips where uterine activity was significantly inhibited by glyceryl trinitrate.
RESULTS	Glyceryl trinitrate reduced the amplitude and frequency of spontaneous uterine contractions in a concentration-dependent manner, although the sensitivity of the myometrial strips varied considerably from one specimen to another.
RESULTS	The concentration of glyceryl trinitrate producing complete inhibition of myometrial contractions ranged from 44-705 microM.
RESULTS	In the presence of glyceryl trinitrate which markedly depressed spontaneous contractions, oxytocin (20 mU/mL), ergometrine (6.15 microM) and prostaglandin F2alpha (6.15 microM) were capable of reversing the uterine activity to either higher than or the untreated level of contractility.
CONCLUSIONS	This study demonstrates that glyceryl trinitrate is a potent uterine relaxant in vitro and that the tocolytic effect could be reversed with ease by oxytocics.

###11236113
OBJECTIVE	To determine whether prophylactic antibiotic administration using cefoxitin at the time of elective caesarean section significantly reduces infectious morbidity.
METHODS	A tertiary teaching hospital in a large urban city in South Africa.
POPULATION	Women undergoing elective caesarean section.
METHODS	A prospective, double-blind randomised placebo-controlled trial.
METHODS	Four hundred and eighty women undergoing elective caesarean section had cefoxitin or placebo administration after umbilical cord clamping.
METHODS	Postpartum complications including febrile morbidity, wound infection, endometritis, urinary tract infection, pneumonia and transient postpartum fever were recorded, as were the duration of hospital stay and the need for therapeutic antibiotics.
RESULTS	Wound infection was the most common complication occurring in 13.3% and 12.5% of women in the placebo and cefoxitin groups, respectively.
RESULTS	Prophylactic antibiotics did not decrease febrile morbidity, wound infection, endometritis, urinary tract infection and pneumonia.
RESULTS	Women who received cefoxitin stayed on average a day less in hospital than those who received placebo (6.9 vs 7.8 days, risk difference 0.94 CI 1.57 - 0.31 days).
RESULTS	Eleven women (4.6%) in the placebo group and eight (3.4%) in the cefoxitin group had microbiological evidence of wound infection.
RESULTS	Staphylococcus aureus was the most common pathogen (43%) isolated.
RESULTS	Similar proportions in both groups (6.3% placebo and 5.1% cefoxitin) required a course of therapeutic antibiotics.
CONCLUSIONS	Antibiotic prophylaxis with cefoxitin in elective caesarean section did not reduce post-operative infectious morbidity in this double-blind randomised placebo controlled trial.

###11232886
OBJECTIVE	To determine the costs associated with the management of hospitalized patients with methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus (MRSA), and to estimate the economic burden associated with MRSA in Canadian hospitals.
METHODS	Patient-specific costs were used to determine the attributable cost of MRSA associated with excess hospitalization and concurrent treatment.
METHODS	Excess hospitalization for infected patients was identified using the Appropriateness Evaluation Protocol, a criterion-based chart review process to determine the need for each day of hospitalization.
METHODS	Concurrent treatment costs were identified through chart review for days in isolation, antimicrobial therapy, and MRSA screening tests.
METHODS	The economic burden to Canadian hospitals was estimated based on 3,167,521 hospital discharges for 1996 and 1997 and an incidence of 4.12 MRSA cases per 1,000 admissions.
METHODS	A tertiary-care, university-affiliated teaching hospital in Toronto, Ontario, Canada.
POPULATION	Inpatients with at least one culture yielding MRSA between April 1996 and March 1998.
RESULTS	A total of 20 patients with MRSA infections and 79 colonized patients (with 94 admissions) were identified.
RESULTS	This represented a rate of 2.9 MRSA cases per 1,000 admissions.
RESULTS	The mean number of additional hospital days attributable to MRSA infection was 14, with 11 admissions having at least 1 attributable day.
RESULTS	The total attributable cost to treat MRSA infections was $287,200, or $14,360 per patient The cost for isolation and management of colonized patients was $128,095, or $1,363 per admission.
RESULTS	Costs for MRSA screening in the hospital were $109,813.
RESULTS	Assuming an infection rate of 10% to 20%, we determined the costs associated with MRSA in Canadian hospitals to be $42 million to $59 million annually.
CONCLUSIONS	These results indicate that there is a substantial economic burden associated with MRSA in Canadian hospitals.
CONCLUSIONS	These costs will continue to rise if the incidence of MRSA increases further.

###11232884
OBJECTIVE	To identify risk factors associated with tuberculin reactivity in healthcare workers (HCWs).
METHODS	Cross-sectional survey of tuberculin reactivity (2 TU of purified protein derivative (PPD) RT23, using the Mantoux two-step test).
METHODS	Two general hospitals located in a region with a high prevalence of tuberculosis and high bacille Calmette-Guerin (BCG) coverage.
POPULATION	Volunteer sample of HCWs.
RESULTS	605 HCWs were recruited: 71.2% female; mean age, 36.4 (standard deviation [SD], 8.2) years; 48.9% nurses, 10.4% physicians, 26.8% administrative personnel; mean time of employment, 10.9 (SD, 6.7) years.
RESULTS	PPD reactivity (> or =10 mm) was found in 390 (64.5%).
RESULTS	Multivariate analysis revealed an association of tuberculin reactivity with occupational exposure in the hospital: participation in autopsies (odds ratio [OR], 9.3; 95% confidence interval [CI95], 2.1-40.5; P=.003. ), more than 1 year of employment (OR, 2.4; CI95, 1.1-5.0; P=.02), work in the emergency or radiology departments (OR, 2.0; CI95, 1.03-3.81; P=.04), being physicians or nurses (OR, 1.5; CI95, 1.04-2.11; P=.03), age (OR, 1.04; CI95, 1.02-1.07 per year of age; P<.001), and BCG scar (OR, 2.1; CI95, 1.2-3.4; P=.005).
CONCLUSIONS	Although the studied population has a high baseline prevalence of tuberculosis infection and high coverage of BCG vaccination, nosocomial risk factors associated with PPD reactivity were identified as professional risks; strict early preventive measures must be implemented accordingly.

###11232659
BACKGROUND	Manufactures of orthopaedic fracture implants have turned to titanium in a pure form and an alloy during the past ten to fifteen years.
BACKGROUND	Although primarily because of the biomechanical properties of this metal, concern for allergy to nickel and chromium ions in stainless steel was a factor in these decisions.
OBJECTIVE	To document the incidence of baseline sensitivity to metal ions and the incidence of conversion to sensitivity to one of three ions in stainless steel in a population of trauma patients at a Level I trauma center.
METHODS	Prospective, consecutive patient series.
METHODS	Level I trauma center.
POPULATION	Patients eighteen years of age and older with no history of metallic implants were recruited for this study between October 1995 and July 1997.
POPULATION	Four hundred ninety-three patients had a Finn chamber device with chromium, nickel, and cobalt ions, which were read using a photographic scale on day three.
POPULATION	Two hundred forty-two of these patients had placement of a second patch, at a mean interval of 187 days (range 45 to 589 days).
INTERVENTION	Internal fixation of fracture or osteotomy with metal implant.
INTERVENTION	MAIN OUTCOME MANAGEMENT: Cutaneous reactivity to metal ions.
RESULTS	Prevalence of sensitivity to chromium was 0.2 percent, to nickel 1.3 percent, and to cobalt 1.8 percent.
RESULTS	Rates for conversion from a negative to positive status were 2.7 percent for chromium, 3.8 percent for nickel, and 3.8 percent for cobalt.
RESULTS	Rates for desensitization (i.e., converting from a positive to negative status) were 2.1 percent for nickel and 3.8 percent for cobalt.
CONCLUSIONS	The prevalence of sensitivity to nickel, cobalt, and chromium is apparently low.
CONCLUSIONS	Similarly, internal fixation devices composed of stainless steel appear to result in an equal incidence of conversion to metal ionic sensitivity and desensitization to metal ions.
CONCLUSIONS	It is conceivable that cutaneous sensitivity is not representative of deep immune response.

###11230623
OBJECTIVE	To gather clinicopathologic data on subcutaneous granuloma annulare (SGA), a subtype of granuloma annulare that occurs exclusively in children and is histologically similar to rheumatoid nodules.
METHODS	Retrospective record review.
POPULATION	Children <10 years old in whom SGA, deep granuloma annulare, or necrobiotic granuloma was diagnosed at the Mayo Clinic (Rochester, MN) from 1983 to 1998.
RESULTS	Thirty-four patients (21 girls and 13 boys; average age at diagnosis: 4.6 years) were found to have SGA.
RESULTS	The lesions predominantly occurred in the lower extremity, especially in a pretibial location.
RESULTS	Local recurrence within 1 month to 7 years was documented in 38.2%; recurrence at other locations was documented in 14.7%.
RESULTS	Average follow-up was 60 months; during follow-up, no patients developed signs or symptoms of rheumatologic disease.
RESULTS	Insulin-dependent diabetes mellitus was diagnosed in 2 patients, 1 before the development of SGA and 1 after it by 1 month.
CONCLUSIONS	SGA is a lesion that presents as subcutaneous nodules on the lower extremities, hands, or scalp in young children.
CONCLUSIONS	Recurrence is common but usually does not warrant additional biopsy.

###11230068
OBJECTIVE	To describe women's understanding of a negative smear test result when presented using the term "normal smear result," as required by the NHS cervical screening programme, and to evaluate the impact on understanding of different ways of presenting the residual risk inherent in such a result.
METHODS	Experimental questionnaire based study.
INTERVENTION	Participants were asked to imagine that they had received a normal smear result.
INTERVENTION	The meaning of this result was then presented using different combinations of three different expressions of residual risk of having or developing cervical cancer over the next five years: a verbal probability of absolute risk (low risk), a numerical probability of absolute risk (1 in 5000), or a numerical probability of risk relative to an unscreened woman (five times lower).
POPULATION	1027 women aged 20 to 64.
RESULTS	When informed only that their smear result was normal, 52% (80 of 153 women) of participants correctly understood that this entailed a residual risk of cervical cancer, compared with 70% (107 of 152) given the additional sentence explaining the meaning of a normal smear result using a verbal probability of absolute risk (difference 18%; 95% confidence interval 7% to 29%).
RESULTS	Additionally, explaining the results using a numerical probability of absolute or relative risk did not increase the proportion who correctly understood that there was a residual risk of cervical cancer.
CONCLUSIONS	NHS policy for reporting normal smears needs to change to make it a definite requirement that the reporting of a "normal smear result" is accompanied by a sentence stating that this means a low risk for having or developing cervical cancer in the next five years.

###11230063
OBJECTIVE	To examine young people's awareness of and involvement with tobacco marketing and to determine the association, if any, between this and their smoking behaviour.
METHODS	Cross sectional, quantitative survey, part interview and part self completion, administered in respondents' homes.
METHODS	North east England.
POPULATION	Stratified random sample of 629 young people aged 15 and 16 years who had "opted in" to research through a postal consent procedure.
RESULTS	There was a high level of awareness of and involvement in tobacco marketing among the 15-16 year olds sampled in the study: around 95% were aware of advertising and all were aware of some method of point of sale marketing.
RESULTS	Awareness of and involvement with tobacco marketing were both significantly associated with being a smoker: for example, 30% (55/185) of smokers had received free gifts through coupons in cigarette packs, compared with 11% (21/199) of non-smokers (P<0.001).
RESULTS	When other factors known to be linked with teenage smoking were held constant, awareness of coupon schemes, brand stretching, and tobacco marketing in general were all independently associated with current smoking status.
CONCLUSIONS	Teenagers are aware of, and are participating in, many forms of tobacco marketing, and both awareness and participation are associated with current smoking status.
CONCLUSIONS	This suggests that the current voluntary regulations designed to protect young people from smoking are not working, and that statutory regulations are required.

###11228451
OBJECTIVE	To determine the safety and efficacy of submucosal radiofrequency uvulopalatoplasty.
OBJECTIVE	This procedure uses fixed, predetermined parameters of application without the concomitant monitoring of tissue impedance and temperature in the submucosal radiofrequency induced ablation and volumetric reduction of the soft palate as a treatment of chronic socially disruptive snoring.
METHODS	This nonrandomized prospective study used preoperative and postoperative questionnaires and visual analogue scales to evaluate snoring and postoperative pain.
METHODS	Treatments were performed on an outpatient basis in a community-based clinic.
POPULATION	Nineteen patients sought treatment for chronic socially disruptive snoring, without complaints of excessive daytime somnolence, who met the predetermined criteria of a respiratory distress index of less than 20.
INTERVENTION	Radiofrequency energy (3.8 MHZ) was applied to the submucosal tissues of the soft palate through a specially designed needle electrode with a predetermined wattage and treatment time.
INTERVENTION	The number of treatments ranged from 1 to 6 (mean, 3.1).
INTERVENTION	Resolution of snoring or diminution of snoring, to the point where it was no longer bothersome to the patients' bedmate, was considered the successful endpoint of the treatments.
RESULTS	Sixteen patients were successfully treated and satisfied with the results.
RESULTS	Three patients had only mild or moderate improvement in snoring despite multiple treatments.
RESULTS	Postoperative pain was rated as mild (< or = 3 on the visual analogue scale) in 86% of treatments.
RESULTS	In 8 (14%) of 58 treatments, mucosal injury was inadvertently sustained, but all healed without complications.
CONCLUSIONS	Submucosal radiofrequency uvulopalatoplasty, using the described technique, appears to be a safe, effective, and well-tolerated treatment for chronic socially disruptive snoring.
CONCLUSIONS	These initial results indicate that the concomitant monitoring of tissue impedance and tissue temperature during radiofrequency ablation and volumetric reduction of the soft palate is not necessary and adds significantly to the cost of the procedure.

###11227834
OBJECTIVE	To examine during recent years the rate of work resumption after an acute myocardial infarction or coronary artery bypass surgery, and to analyse variables that predicted return to work.
METHODS	Referral centre for cardiac rehabilitation at the university hospital in Gent.
POPULATION	227 consecutive patients (90 after a first AMI; 137 after a first CABG) were selected for participation.
POPULATION	All patients were less than 60 years old and in a social state that still allowed return to work.
POPULATION	During hospitalisation, a set of questionnaires, validated as well as self-developed, was presented, measuring psychological and social variables.
POPULATION	Medical variables were collected from the medical records.
POPULATION	One year later, a follow-up questionnaire was sent by mail, measuring return to work, reasons for not returning, morbidity, and psychological well-being.
RESULTS	Return to work was observed in 185/222 (83.3%) of the total study group; 75/86 (87.2%) of the AMI patients and 110/136 (80.8%) of the CABG patients.
RESULTS	The mean delay for return to work was 14.8 weeks.
RESULTS	After one year, patients who returned to work, showed more positive affect, less negative affect, less somatic complaints and less cognitive complaints.
RESULTS	This better psychological profile was not affected by the morbidity score.
RESULTS	Variables predicting return to work in CABG patients were different from those in AMI patients.
RESULTS	Only two medical variables could be retained in CABG patients (good left venticular function and a larger degree of revascularisation).
RESULTS	Mainly psychological variables had predictive power (trust, job security, positive expectations concerning return, no attribution to stress, less somatic complaints, less physical exertion of the job).
CONCLUSIONS	Return to work remains one of the main issues in cardiac rehabilitation after AMI or CABG.
CONCLUSIONS	If resumption is sufficiently emphasized, a high success rate can be achieved.
CONCLUSIONS	This approach should include a psychosocial strategy starting already during hospitalisation.

###11227163
BACKGROUND	Cyanoacrylates are contained in nail glues, instant glues, and tissue adhesives such as Histoacryl.
BACKGROUND	Cyanoacrylates may inadvertently contaminate the conjunctival sac and cause massive loss of visual function and serious discomfort to the patient.
POPULATION	Over a 10-year period five patients with a cyanoacrylate lesion of the ocular surface have been observed.
POPULATION	Four were injured by instant glue and one by Histoacryl.
POPULATION	Therapy included moderate rinsing.
POPULATION	In the most severely affected patient parts of the glue were removed mechanically; spontaneous rejection of the glue was awaited in the other four.
POPULATION	After the glue had been rejected, four patients showed corneal erosion which healed without problems.
POPULATION	Visual function returned to normal in all patients, and there was no persistent tissue damage.
CONCLUSIONS	Cyanoacrylates exert no substantial thermal, chemical, or toxic effects on the ocular surface when inadvertently entering the conjunctival sac.
CONCLUSIONS	Cyanoacrylate trauma therefore has a very favorable prognosis.
CONCLUSIONS	The conservative approach of awaiting spontaneous glue rejection is appropriate.

###11225470
BACKGROUND	Most patients with terminal renal failure show arterial hypertension.
BACKGROUND	In addition to casual blood pressure measurements in the clinic, home blood pressure measurement is recommended for these patients to control arterial blood pressure.
POPULATION	The study was performed in children with hemodialysis (HD; n = 11), peritoneal dialysis (PD; n = 14) or after renal transplantation (NTX; n = 21) from one department of Pediatric Nephrology.
POPULATION	We performed a retrospective analysis of home blood pressure values from patients' diaries.
METHODS	The average number of blood pressure measurements per day and the mean blood pressure values were calculated from the blood pressure data documented during one month at home.
METHODS	Single measurements above the 95th percentile for height and gender were defined to be hypertensive and the frequency as percentage of all documented values was calculated.
RESULTS	Four patients did not document any blood pressure values at home.
RESULTS	The other patients documented an average of 2.3 measurements per day.
RESULTS	Systolic hypertension was found in 7% of patients defined by home BP measurements compared to 30% defined by casual BP measurements.
RESULTS	Prevalence of diastolic hypertension did not differ between both methods (35% vs. 46%).
RESULTS	Mean home BP was significantly higher than values after HD and lower than values before HD.
RESULTS	Mean clinic BP was significantly higher in PD-patients compared to home BP.
RESULTS	Home and clinic blood pressure values did not differ in patients after renal transplantation.
RESULTS	The mean percentage of hypertensive readings for systolic BP was 5 and for diastolic BP 39%.
CONCLUSIONS	Blood pressure measurement at home is performed reliably by most children and adolescents with chronic renal failure and shows lower values than clinic BP in many patients.
CONCLUSIONS	It is an important method for control of blood pressure and a valuable supplement to 24 h blood pressure monitoring.

###11225400
BACKGROUND	Ocular involvement in panarteritis nodosa (PAN) has been reported to occur in 10 to 20% of patients.
BACKGROUND	In 3 patients with acute visual disturbance we point out unusual findings.
POPULATION	Case 1.
POPULATION	A 40-year-old man initially presented with papilledema together with partial optic atrophy in both eyes, later polyneuropathy, gangrene of the toes and myalgic pains developed.
POPULATION	Caliber changes in the small arteries in the liver were seen angiographically and recognized as signs of PAN.
POPULATION	Under treatment with cyclophosphamide und prednisone no relapse occurred during a follow-up of 2 years.
POPULATION	Case 2.
POPULATION	In a 67-year-old man who suffered from arterial hypertension and coronary heart disease, central retinal artery occlusion occurred, at first in the left and then later in the right eye.
POPULATION	The clinically suspected diagnosis of PAN (arterial hypertension, myalgia, polyneuropathy) was confirmed by a muscle biopsy.
POPULATION	During a follow-up of 4 years--including treatment with prednisone and cyclophosphamide--no relapse occurred.
POPULATION	Case 3.
POPULATION	A 16-year-old adolescent with throbbing headaches and a thickened right temporal artery reported visual disturbances.
POPULATION	These were due to an inflammation of choroidal vessels of the right eye appearing as an initial sign of PAN.
POPULATION	Histology revealed a necrotising arteritis of the temporal artery.
POPULATION	He presented with signs of Raynaud's disease, cachexia and arterial hypertension.
POPULATION	Multiple vasculitic changes were detected by aorto-arteriography.
POPULATION	Five months after the visual deterioration an anterior spinal artery syndrome with quadriplegia developed.
POPULATION	After a follow-up of 2 years and treatment with prednisone und cyclophosphamide, he still had paralysis of both legs.
POPULATION	The visual acuity was 1.0 in each eye.
CONCLUSIONS	PAN should be considered in differential diagnosis in patients with acute inflammatory signs of the optic nerve head, the choroid and/or the retina together with general signs of the disease.
CONCLUSIONS	If the disease is suspected, a muscle biopsy is indicated.

###11223705
OBJECTIVE	To evaluate the accuracy of abdominal circumference (AC) estimation of macrosomia early in labor and whether a cutoff value could be detected.
METHODS	A prospective clinical trial.
METHODS	The Department of Obstetrics and Gynecology, Cairo University.
POPULATION	One hundred pregnant females presenting in early labor with clinical impression of macrosomia were examined by ultrasound, and those babies with abdominal circumference more or equal to 35 cm were recruited for the study.
INTERVENTION	Every woman was considered as her own control, and fetal weight was calculated using the formula of Shepard et al. The accuracy of abdominal circumference in prediction of macrosomia was evaluated using the Receiver operating characteristic curve.
RESULTS	A cutoff value of AC > or =37 cm was found to have a sensitivity of 77%, a specificity of 75%, a positive likelihood ratio of 3.1 and a negative likelihood ratio of 0.3.
CONCLUSIONS	Intrapartum ultrasonographic evaluation of abdominal circumference for suspected macrosomic babies in early labor is an easy, practical method that should be adopted in decision making.

###11220704
OBJECTIVE	As breastfeeding is suggested to protect against diabetes mellitus we decided to investigate whether the seasonal variation of month of birth of diabetic children, with more diabetes in children born in summer, can be explained to some extent by a seasonal variation of exclusive breastfeeding.
POPULATION	A population-based group of 297 children who had been diagnosed with diabetes mellitus before the age of 15 years was compared with 792 matched healthy subjects.
RESULTS	There was no difference in duration of breast-feeding between children who later got diabetes and the controls.
RESULTS	Children (both diabetics and controls) born during the summer were exclusively breastfed for a mean period of 2.2 months.
RESULTS	Corresponding figures for children born during winter were 2.8 months (p<0.04), spring 2.5 months (n.s.) and autumn 2.7 months (p<0.05).
RESULTS	Seasonality was most pronounced in children who developed diabetes between the ages of 10 and 15 years.
CONCLUSIONS	These results indicate that children born during the summer, who have increased risk of developing diabetes mellitus, have also been exclusively breastfed for a shorter time.

###11218974
OBJECTIVE	Main: to assess the change in coronary risk (CR) in adults after 8 years of their involvement in the Programme of Preventive Activities and Health Promotion (PAPPS).
OBJECTIVE	Secondary: to determine the level of anti-tetanus vaccination reached and patients' compliance with activities.
METHODS	"Before and after" intervention study without random allocation.
METHODS	A general medical clinic at a health centre.
POPULATION	429 patients (204 men, 225 women) between 30 and 65 monitored for 8 years, recruited by active search for cases at daily consultations.
INTERVENTION	Blood pressure, cholesterol, weight, tobacco habit, alcohol intake, anti-tetanus vaccination state, CR calculation at 10 years on the Framingham scale, and degree of compliance with activities were all determined at the start, at 4 years and at 8 years.
INTERVENTION	Data was obtained from the clinical notes.
RESULTS	Total population: a 0.8 drop in CR (CI difference: 0.4-1.2), equivalent to 8.5% of the initial figure. 64.4% increase in correct anti-tetanus vaccination (CI difference: 59.9-69).
RESULTS	Compliance with activities at the start and after eight years: pressure 100%, 71%; tobacco consumption 99.5%, 71%; cholesterol determination 89%, 64%.
RESULTS	Initially high CR sub-group: 6.7 drop of CR at 8 years (CI difference: 4.9-8.5), equivalent to 24.8% of the initial figure.
CONCLUSIONS	In the total population, the CR drop found was not clinically significant, whereas in the initially high CR sub-group the drop was.
CONCLUSIONS	There should probably be an active search made for patients with high CR and action taken on them.

###11218969
OBJECTIVE	To perform an economics evaluation of lipid-lowering therapy with atorvastatin and simvastatin in patients with hypercholesterolemia in primary care setting.
METHODS	Cost-effectiveness analysis (CEA) has been carried out by means of an open, random, prospective, "real world" study, with hypercholesterolemic patients (total cholesterol [TC] > 240 mg/dl and cLDL > 160 mg/dl).
POPULATION	A total of 92 patients were included (44.8% males), with a mean age of 64.9 +/- 9.4 years old (mean +/- standard deviation). 41.4% were diabetics, 62.1% hypertensives and 16.1% smokers.
INTERVENTION	Patients were allocated to simvastatin 20 mg/day (44) and atorvastatin. 10 mg/day (48) for 6 months.
INTERVENTION	ASSESSMENT AND MAIN RESULTS: Both therapies reduced significantly cLDL, TC and triglycerides at the end of the study.
INTERVENTION	Atorvastatin reduced lipids faster than simvastatin at 3 months (p < 0.05), but significant differences could not be observed at 6 months.
INTERVENTION	Atorvastatin reduced cLDL levels by 21.5 +/- 13.2% and 23.8 +/- 13.9% at 3 and 6 months, respectively, versus 16.4 +/- 14.2% and 22.8 +/- 10.8% with simvastatin.
INTERVENTION	By these reductions, 54.2% of patients treated with atorvastatin and 50.0% of those allocated to simvastatin reached therapeutic goals of cLDL control.
INTERVENTION	Atorvastatin 10 mg was more cost-effective than simvastatin 20 mg; 95,406 versus 101,335 pts per patient reaching therapeutic goals, respectively, which means that simvastatin need an extra cost of 24,833 pts per patient reaching therapeutic goals to be as efficient as atorvastatin.
INTERVENTION	Sensitivity analysis to control for uncertainty confirmed the results of cost-effectiveness analysis.
CONCLUSIONS	Both statins were effective as lipid-lowering agents.
CONCLUSIONS	However, atorvastatin 10 mg was more efficient than simvastatin 20 mg due to a better cost-effectiveness ratio.

###11218622
BACKGROUND	In recent years several epidemiological surveys have reported the association between Helicobacter Pylori (HP), chronic ischemic heart disease (CHD) and cerebrovascular disease (CVD).
BACKGROUND	None involved the geriatric population in which these diseases are prevalent.
METHODS	We investigated the HP seropositivity prevalence in a group of over 75-year-old subjects recruited among all patients consecutively admitted for various diseases in an Internal Medicine Department during a period of 9 months.
INTERVENTION	We measured HP IgG antibodies employing a commercially available kit based on reverse flow chromatography highly feasible and simple to perform and we investigated all patients for past or present CHD/CVD.
RESULTS	From our data, the prevalence of seropositivity to Hp does not differ from other wider age stratified studies; more in detail; in the over 90-year-old patients we found the same HP seropositivity prevalence as in younger group but were not able to confirm the progressive lowering or seroprevalence in extreme geriatric age.
CONCLUSIONS	No clear correlation between HP seroprevalence and CHD and CVD was found, but a link with CHD seems possible for the determinant (age) is common to both conditions.

###11216570
OBJECTIVE	To explore further the mechanisms leading to immune deficiency in chronic renal failure and the role of dialysis treatment in these mechanisms.
METHODS	Cross-sectional and longitudinal analysis.
POPULATION	We studied 39 children treated with peritoneal dialysis (PD), 23 children treated with hemodialysis (HD), 33 children not yet dialyzed [chronic renal failure (CRF)], and 27 healthy children.
POPULATION	Peritoneal cells were also obtained from PD children for analysis.
METHODS	White blood cells (WBCs) were isolated from blood and peritoneal dialysis effluent by centrifugation.
METHODS	The number of CD2+, CD4+, and CD8+ T cells, B cells, and natural killer cells were measured by flow cytometry.
RESULTS	The total peripheral blood lymphocyte count was lower in PD children (2.6 x 10(9)/L), HD children (2.1 x 10(9)/L), and CRF children (2.0 x 10(9)/L) compared with healthy children (3.1 x 10(9)/L, p < 0.05).
RESULTS	The B lymphocyte count was also lower in PD children (0.34 x 10(9)/L), HD children (0.22 x 10(9)/L), and CRF children (0.33 x 10(9)/L) compared with healthy children (0.52 x 10(9)/L, p < 0.01).
RESULTS	Numbers of CD4+ T cells were not different, but numbers of CD8+ T cells were lower in PD children (0.56 x 10(9)/L), HD children (0.63 x 10(9)/L), and CRF children (0.53 x 10(9)/L) compared with healthy children (0.77 x 10(9)/L, p < 0.05).
RESULTS	The count of natural killer cells was lower in PD children (0.21 x 10(9)/L), HD children (0.17 x 10(9)/L), and CRF children (0.18 x 10(9)/L) compared with healthy children (0.50 x 10(9)/L, p < 0.0001).
RESULTS	The CD4/CD8 ratio of lymphocytes in peritoneal effluent was 0.8 versus 1.9 in peripheral blood (p < 0.001).
RESULTS	The CD2/CD19 ratio was not different.
RESULTS	The cell subsets remained stable during the first year of PD treatment.
RESULTS	The CD2/CD19 ratio in peritoneal effluent was higher in children with a peritonitis incidence > or = 1 per year.
CONCLUSIONS	The reduced numbers of B lymphocytes, CD8+ T cells, and natural killer cells found in CRF children, dialyzed or not, may favor the frequent occurrence of infections.

###11216559
BACKGROUND	The peritoneal equilibration test (PET) is a useful assessment of peritoneal function in continuous ambulatory peritoneal dialysis (CAPD) patients.
BACKGROUND	However, the natural course of longitudinal change in peritoneal transport is not well defined.
POPULATION	We studied 105 unselected CAPD patients.
POPULATION	Average age at enrollment was 50.7 +/- 11.3 years.
METHODS	A PET was performed at enrollment.
METHODS	Peritoneal transport was expressed as dialysate-to-plasma creatinine ratio at 4 hours (DIP).
METHODS	Fibrosing factors and mesothelial cell markers, including TGFbeta, epidermal growth factor (EGF), platelet-derived growth factor (PDGF), hyaluronan, and cancer antigen 125 (CA125), were measured in overnight peritoneal dialysate effluent (PDE).
METHODS	Patients were followed for two years.
METHODS	Peritonitis episodes were recorded.
METHODS	Severe peritonitis was defined as an episode that required catheter removal or antibiotic therapy for more than 3 weeks.
METHODS	After two years, 75 patients were still alive and on CAPD.
RESULTS	The PET was repeated in 64 patients, of whom 35 were male and 9 had diabetes.
RESULTS	The change in D/P over two years was represented as AD/P.
RESULTS	No significant change in peritoneal transport was seen after two years (D/P: 0.56 +/- 0.12 vs 0.55 +/- 0.13).
RESULTS	A centripetal pattern of change in D/P was observed.
RESULTS	The deltaD/P had normal distribution and was inversely correlated with D/P at baseline (r = -0.427, p < 0.005).
RESULTS	Both results suggest a regression-to-mean phenomenon.
RESULTS	The deltaD/P had no significant correlation with the total number of peritonitis episodes (Spearman r = 0.052, p = 0.74), but after severe peritonitis, affected patients had higher deltaD/P than patients who experienced no severe infection (0.040 +/- 0.136 vs -0.032 +/- 0.120, p < 0.05).
RESULTS	For patients with no episodes of severe peritonitis (n = 47), deltaD/P was weakly correlated with baseline TGFbeta level (r = -0.506, p < 0.01).
RESULTS	No correlation was seen between the levels of other fibrosing factors and change in peritoneal transport.
CONCLUSIONS	Our findings suggest that the centripetal change of peritoneal transport probably reflects a regression-to-mean phenomenon.
CONCLUSIONS	Peritoneal transport increases after severe peritonitis.
CONCLUSIONS	The role of TGFbeta levels in PDE with regard to longitudinal change in peritoneal transport requires further study.

###11216142
OBJECTIVE	To determine maternal weight gain curve for twin gestation and to examine the risk of preterm birth associated with rates of weight gain (kg/week) to and from 24 weeks to delivery.
POPULATION	This study involved analysis data collected retrospectively on a cohort of women who received prenatal care from Outpatient Department for Women in Czechów district in Lublin, from 1986 to 1998.
POPULATION	The study sample included 39 twin pregnancies. 12 (30.8%) women delivering twins before 37 weeks.
METHODS	Gestational age was estimated from first day of last menstrual period.
METHODS	Information on prepregnancy weight was obtained by interview at the first prenatal visit.
METHODS	Weight gain was expressed in several ways: 1) For the descriptive analysis of weight gain by gestational age, the woman's pregravid weight was subtracted from the weight measured at each visit.
METHODS	2) The weekly rate of weight gain was calculated by dividing the amount of weight gained between visits by the number of weeks during the time interval.
METHODS	Then we calculated the mean weekly rates before and after 24 weeks for each individual.
RESULTS	Weight curve in group of women pregnant with twins showed similar linear trends.
RESULTS	In women delivering twins before 37 weeks, the rate of weekly gain to and from 24 weeks to delivery was lower (statistically nonsignificant) when compared with mothers delivering at term (0.38 versus 0.53 and 0.58 versus 0.69 kg/week, respectively).
CONCLUSIONS	The results suggested, that spontaneous preterm delivery was associated with lower rate of weight gain before and after 24 weeks.

###11214774
OBJECTIVE	To compare the elimination of fluconazole by continuous veno-venous haemodiafiltration (CVVHD) and continuous veno-venous haemofiltration (CVVH) at different dosages.
INTERVENTION	Patients received doses of 400 mg (n=3), 600 mg (n=1) or 800 mg (n=2) fluconazole as a short-time infusion once a day.
INTERVENTION	Patients underwent CVVHD the first day and CVVH the second day.
INTERVENTION	CVVHD and CVVH were performed using an acrylonitrile hollow-fibre filter at a constant blood flow of 90 ml/min and a substitution flow of 1000 ml/h (predilution).
INTERVENTION	During CVVHD, the dialysate flow was 1000 ml/h.
INTERVENTION	Ultrafiltration rates were 1158+/-90.5 ml/h during CVVHD and 1167+/-81.6 ml/h.
INTERVENTION	Serum and ultrafiltrate/dialysate concentrations of fluconazole were determined on nine occasions over 24 h.
POPULATION	Six critically ill patients with acute renal failure (ARF) and serious fungal infection.
RESULTS	Extracorporeal clearance (CVVHD 30.5+/-6.0 ml/min, CVVH 17.5+/-4.0 ml/min) and total clearance of fluconazole (CVVHD 37.9+/-4.4 ml/min, CVVH 25.3+/-6.5 ml/min) were significantly higher during CVVHD (P < 0.05).
RESULTS	During CVVHD, the sieving coefficient (S(CVVHD)) was 0.88 (range 0.54-1) and the elimination half-life (t1/2) was 14.8-35.1 h. During CVVH, the S(CVVH) was 0.96 (range 0.56-1.02) and t1/2 was 24.0-51.6 h.
CONCLUSIONS	A daily dosage of 400-800 mg fluconazole is recommended in the treatment of life-threatening fungal infections in critically ill patients undergoing CVVHD since the clearance of CVVHD may considerably exceed the clearance in patients with normal renal function, which is about 20 ml/min.
CONCLUSIONS	Drug monitoring is highly recommended for these patients.

###11214661
OBJECTIVE	To investigate factors that influence length of stay (LOS) in patients hospitalized for transient ischemic attacks (TIAs).
OBJECTIVE	This may help to reduce unnecessary days of hospitalization.
METHODS	Retrospective study.
METHODS	A large, non-academic general hospital.
POPULATION	All patients hospitalized for TIAs during the years 1996-1998 were retrospectively studied.
POPULATION	We analyzed the relationship between demographic, clinical and management features and LOS.
RESULTS	We studied 157 patients.
RESULTS	Median LOS was 8 days (range, 1 to 21 days).
RESULTS	Statistical analysis showed that female sex, being hospitalized in 1996, hospitalization from Wednesday through Saturday, and second-level cardiological, neuroradiological and miscellaneous investigations significantly increased LOS.
CONCLUSIONS	The LOS of patients with TIAs may be reduced through measures that accelerate the diagnostic work-up and by scheduling the admission to the first days of the week, when this is possibile.

###11213011
OBJECTIVE	To compare the results of removing mature teratoma with laparoscopy or without laparoscopy.
METHODS	A prospective, randomised trial.
METHODS	Medical centre.
POPULATION	Seventy-nine women with mature teratomas identified using results of ultrasound examinations and biochemical markers.
INTERVENTION	Cystectomy with laparoscopic approach or without laparoscopic approach through a culdotomy.
METHODS	Patients were randomly assigned to have their cysts removed via vaginal cystectomy without laparoscopy (n = 37, Group A) or laparoscopic cystectomy via culdotomy opening (n = 42, Group B).
METHODS	Inclusion criteria were history of vaginal delivery, no previous abdominal surgery, no history of pelvic inflammatory disease, no medical illness, and no presenting symptoms.
METHODS	Eight women randomised to Group A withdrew before surgery.
METHODS	The laparoscopically resected tumours were each put into a cellulose bag, and tumours without laparoscopic-assistance were removed directly via the vagina.
RESULTS	Blood loss in Group A (88 +/- 37 ml) was significantly more than that in Group B (64 +/- 20 ml, P = 0.000).
RESULTS	The post-operative recovery times were 20 and 17 hours, respectively (P = 0.030).
RESULTS	The rates of successful surgery were 58.6 and 97.6%, respectively (P = 0.002).
RESULTS	The spillage rates were 44.8% and 19.0%, respectively (P = 0.006).
RESULTS	There were no significant differences in tumour size, patient age, and operative time between groups.
CONCLUSIONS	Cystectomy without assistance of laparoscopy could be applied to manage mature teratoma of the ovary; however, because of the difficulty of this technique, we had high percentages of tumour spillage and more blood loss during operation and a high percentage of patients who required conversion to laparotomy compared with laparoscopic cystectomy.
CONCLUSIONS	We favoured laparoscopically assisted cystectomy to manage mature teratoma.

###11213003
OBJECTIVE	To investigate the effect of oral misoprostol in dosages varying from 200 microg to 800 microg on postpartum uterine contractility and to establish their side effects.
METHODS	A prospective descriptive study.
POPULATION	Fifty-seven women who delivered vaginally after spontaneous labours not requiring augmentation.
METHODS	Within 5 minutes of delivery of the placenta, a calibrated Gaeltec catheter with an intrauterine pressure transducer at its tip was inserted transcervically into the uterine cavity.
METHODS	Cumulative uterine activity was recorded for 30 minutes in each woman before administering the oral misoprostol tablets and continued for a further 90 minutes after its administration.
METHODS	Thus each woman acted as her own control regarding changes in uterine contractility.
METHODS	Uterine activity was recorded on a Sonicaid Meridian fetal monitor, which measures active contraction area automatically.
METHODS	The incidence of side effects was also recorded.
RESULTS	There was no statistical difference (P = 0.887) in the adjusted mean difference in cumulative uterine activity following all the doses of oral misoprostol, compared with intramuscular syntometrine, the largest difference being seen in oral misoprostol 200 microg (adjusted mean difference -2282 kPas s, 95% CI -7954 to 3390 kPas s).
RESULTS	The mean onset of action of oral misoprostol (6.1, SD 2.1 min) was significantly slower than that of intramuscular syntometrine (3.2, SD 1.5 min; P = 0.002), but their durations of action were similar (P = 0.637).
RESULTS	In the misoprostol group the commonest side effects were shivering (36%) and a rise in body temperature above 38 degrees C (40%).
RESULTS	In the syntometrine group, the most commonly observed side effect was moderate uterine pain (nine out of ten women) and a rise in diastolic blood pressure of 20 mmHg (two out of ten women).
CONCLUSIONS	The results of this study show that oral misoprostol has a definite uterotonic effect on the postpartum uterus.
CONCLUSIONS	At doses of 200 microg to 400 microg, oral misoprostol has a similar uterotonic effect to intramuscular syntometrine.
CONCLUSIONS	Higher doses of oral misoprostol are associated with significantly more side effects.

###11212999
OBJECTIVE	To compare the efficacy of oral with vaginal misoprostol for induction of labour at term.
METHODS	Randomised trial.
METHODS	Tertiary Care hospital.
POPULATION	One hundred and sixty-seven women requiring induction of labour.
METHODS	The women were randomised to receive 50 microg of misoprostol orally or vaginally every 6 h until the cervix was favourable for amniotomy, spontaneous rupture of membranes, or active labour occurred.
METHODS	Sample size was calculated with a two-tailed alpha of 0.05 and a power of 95% to detect a 5 h difference in induction-to-delivery time.
METHODS	Student's t test was used for comparison of normally distributed continuous variables and the Mann-Whitney U test was used for non-Gaussian distributed continuous variables.
METHODS	Fisher' s exact and chi2 tests were used for comparison of categorical variables.
METHODS	The main outcome measure was induction to delivery time.
RESULTS	The median induction to delivery time was significantly shorter with vaginal misoprostol (15.7 h range 4.3-55.7), compared with oral misoprostol (23.0 h range 3.2-141.7, P = 0.0013).
RESULTS	The median number of doses was also significantly less in the vaginal misoprostol group, 1 (range 1-3), compared with the oral group, 2 (range 1-8), (P < 0.0001).
RESULTS	The significant differences in outcome held true when nulliparous and multiparous women were analysed separately.
RESULTS	There were no differences between the two routes of administration with respect to rates of hyperstimulation or neonatal asphyxia.
RESULTS	There were more caesarean sections in the vaginal misoprostol group, but the difference was not statistically significant.
CONCLUSIONS	Compared with oral misoprostol, vaginal misoprostol for induction of labour at term results in a shorter induction-to-delivery time, with fewer doses required per patient.
CONCLUSIONS	Vaginal misoprostol may be associated with higher rates of caesarean section than oral misoprostol.

###11212975
OBJECTIVE	Insulin has been suggested to promote myocardial cell growth and the development of left ventricular (LV) hypertrophy.
OBJECTIVE	This study examines the possible relationship between LV mass and insulin sensitivity.
METHODS	Previously untreated non-diabetic hypertensive patients.
POPULATION	Fifty-one patients with mean age 51 +/- 8 years, body mass index (BMI) 25.9 +/- 3.2 kg/m2 and blood pressure 158/102 mmHg were included.
POPULATION	LV mass was determined by echocardiography.
POPULATION	Glucose metabolism was assessed by an euglycemic insulin clamp (40 mU/m2 body surface area/min).
RESULTS	Insulin sensitivity index (MI) and insulin clearance were inversely related to LV mass (r = -0.37, P < 0.01 and -0.33, P < 0.05, respectively) and LV mass indexed to height (r = -0.33, P < 0.05 and -0.29, P < 0.05, respectively).
RESULTS	C-peptide and fasting insulin were related to LV mass (r = 0.33, P < 0.05 and r = 0.36, P < 0.01, respectively) and LV mass indexed to height (r = 0.30, P < 0.05 and r = 0.34, P < 0.05, respectively).
RESULTS	In contrast, when LV mass was indexed by body surface area there was no longer a relation to MI, insulin clearance, C-peptide or fasting insulin.
RESULTS	When adjusting for BMI in a multiple regression analysis, MI and LV mass no longer showed a relation.
RESULTS	Indeed, MI, insulin clearance, C-peptide and insulin were all strongly related to weight and BMI.
CONCLUSIONS	Insulin sensitivity is related to body size in untreated hypertension.
CONCLUSIONS	However, insulin sensitivity is not related to LV mass, if adjusting for body size.
CONCLUSIONS	This does not support a direct growth-promoting effect of insulin on the myocardium.
CONCLUSIONS	Insulin does not appear to be strongly involved in development of hypertensive LV hypertrophy.

###11211167
OBJECTIVE	To describe the change in the use of medication after acute myocardial infarction (AMI) and discuss its possible impact on risk and risk indicators for death.
POPULATION	All patients discharged alive after hospitalization for AMI at Sahlgrenska Hospital (covering half the community of Göteborg, i.e. 250,000 of 500,000 inhabitants) during 1986-1987 (period I) and at Sahlgrenska Hospital and Ostra Hospital (covering the whole community of Göteborg, 500,000 inhabitants) during 1990-1991 (period II).
METHODS	Overall mortality was retrospectively evaluated during 5 years of follow-up.
RESULTS	In all, 740 patients were included in the study during period I and 1448 during period II.
RESULTS	The 5-year mortalities were 44.1% for period I patients and 39.3% for period II patients (P = 0.036).
RESULTS	The relative risk of death for period II patients was 0.78 [95% confidence interval (CI) 0.67-0.89, P = 0.0005] after adjustment for differences at baseline.
RESULTS	There was a significant interaction with a history of congestive heart failure; improvement in duration of survival was found only for patients without such a history.
RESULTS	During period I, only 3% of patients were administered fibrinolytic agents, compared with 33% of patients during period II (P < 0.0001).
RESULTS	During period I, aspirin was prescribed for 13% of patients discharged from hospital compared with 79% during period II.
RESULTS	Other changes in treatment on going from period I to period II included increases in prescription of beta-blockers and angiotensin converting enzyme inhibitors.
RESULTS	After adjustment for various risk indicators for death, relative risk of death for those administered fibrinolytic agents was 0.60 (95% CI 0.18-2.02) for patients in the period-I cohort and 0.68% (95% CI 0.51-0.91) for those in the period-II cohort.
RESULTS	Adjusted relative risk of death for those prescribed aspirin upon discharge from hospital was 0.81 (95% CI 0.52-1.25) for period-I patients and 0.71 (95% CI 0.56-0.91) for period-II patients.
RESULTS	The adjusted relative risk of death for those administered beta-blockers was 0.72 (95% CI 0.55-0.96) for period-I patients and 0.70 (95% CI 0.55-0.90) for period-II patients.
CONCLUSIONS	Increased use of fibrinolytic agents and aspirin for AMI as well as a moderate increase in use of beta-blockers and angiotensin converting enzyme inhibitors was associated with a parallel reduction in age-adjusted mortality during the 5 years after discharge from hospital.
CONCLUSIONS	However, this improvement was seen only for patients without histories of congestive heart failure.

###11210881
OBJECTIVE	The purpose of this study was to investigate the association between deep dental overbite and eustachian tube dysfunction (ETD).
METHODS	Case-control study.
METHODS	Tertiary care pediatric otolaryngology outpatient clinic at the Children's Hospital, Boston, Massachusetts.
POPULATION	105 patients between the ages of 2 and 6 years.
POPULATION	STUDY MEASUREMENTS: Dental overbite, overjet, and occlusal relationships were measured by an observer who was unaware of ETD status.
POPULATION	ETD was defined as having ventilation tubes in place or having the recommendation for ventilation tube placement by an attending pediatric otolaryngologist.
POPULATION	In addition, demographic information and medical and social histories were prospectively recorded.
RESULTS	In a multivariate logistic regression model, children with deep bites were 2.8 times more likely to have ETD than those without deep bites (P = .03).
RESULTS	Other independent risk factors for ETD identified in this model were family history of otitis media (OM) and age less than 3 years.
CONCLUSIONS	Children with deep dental overbites are at a significantly increased risk for developing ETD.

###11208260
OBJECTIVE	To explore whether there is a potential for greater use of research-based information in public health practice in a local setting.
OBJECTIVE	Secondly, if research-based information is relevant, to explore the extent to which this generates questioning behaviour.
METHODS	Qualitative study using focus group discussions, observation and interviews.
METHODS	Public health practices in Norway.
POPULATION	52 public health practitioners.
RESULTS	In general, the public health practitioners had a positive attitude towards research-based information, but believed that they had few cases requiring this type of information.
RESULTS	They did say, however, that there might be a potential for greater use.
RESULTS	During five focus groups and six observation days we identified 28 questions/cases where it would have been appropriate to seek out research evidence according to our definition.
RESULTS	Three of the public health practitioners identified three of these 28 cases as questions for which research-based information could have been relevant.
RESULTS	This gap is interpreted as representing unrecognised information needs.
CONCLUSIONS	There is an unrealised potential in public health practice for more frequent and extensive use of research-based information.
CONCLUSIONS	The practitioners did not appear to reflect on the need for scientific information when faced with new cases and few questions of this type were generated.

###11207636
OBJECTIVE	The aim of the present study was to investigate Norwegian patients with autoimmune polyendocrine syndrome type I (APS I), with respect to occurrence and clinical presentation, reactivity towards different autoantigenes and mutations in the autoimmune regulator (AIRE) gene.
POPULATION	Twenty Norwegian patients from 15 families with APS I (11 males, nine females; mean age 26 years, range 4--54) were included by contacting all major hospitals in Norway.
METHODS	Clinical data was collected from both patients and their physicians by the use of questionnaires and patient records.
METHODS	Autoantibodies were analysed using radioimmunoassays based on antigen synthesized by in vitro transcription and translation.
METHODS	AIRE mutations were determined by DNA sequence analysis.
RESULTS	The prevalence of APS I in Norway was estimated to be about 1 : 80,000 individuals.
RESULTS	We found about the same distribution of disease characteristics as has been reported in Finnish patients.
RESULTS	The diagnosis was delayed in many individuals.
RESULTS	In two thirds of the cases, the patients were admitted in Hospital with acute adrenal insufficiency or hypocalcaemic crisis.
RESULTS	Forty percent of these patients already had one of the main disease manifestations.
RESULTS	Four different mutations in the AIRE gene were found in the Norwegian cohort.
RESULTS	A 13-bp deletion in exon 8 (1085--1097(del)) was the most frequent mutation, present in 22/40 (55%) of the alleles.
RESULTS	Eighty-five percent of the patients had either autoantibodies against 21 hydroxylase or aromatic L-amino acid decarboxylase.
RESULTS	Five of eight women (age > 13 years) had ovarian failure, and all of these had antibodies against side-chain cleavage enzyme (P = 0.0002).
CONCLUSIONS	Norwegian patients with APS I clinically resemble patients from Finland and other European countries.
CONCLUSIONS	The diagnosis APS I must be considered in children and adolescents with chronic mucocutaneous candidiasis, autoimmune adrenocortical failure or hypoparathyroidism in order to avoid fatal complications.
CONCLUSIONS	Analysis of autoantibodies and mutational analysis of the AIRE gene are valuable diagnostic tools, especially in the early stages of the disease.

###11205094
OBJECTIVE	To investigate whether the validity of susceptibility testing in general practice would improve when preceded by an intervention.
INTERVENTION	Instruction in standardised susceptibility testing procedures given by laboratory instructors.
METHODS	Urine specimens containing monocultures of typical uropathogenic bacteria were sent to 23 general practices before and after the intervention.
METHODS	Practices performed susceptibility testing by the Sensicult and the Iso-Res agar methods and the validity of the results before and after the intervention was compared.
METHODS	Results from susceptibility testing at the bacteriological laboratory, Odense University Hospital, were used as gold standard.
RESULTS	The median frequency of correct results increased from 82% to 98% for susceptibility testing based on Sensicult (p = 0.001) and from 90% to 96% based on Iso-Res agar (p = 0.05).
CONCLUSIONS	The validity of susceptibility testing in general practice improves when preceded by instruction in standardised procedures.

###11204807
BACKGROUND	Intractable idiopathic constipation in women is often associated with psychosocial problems.
OBJECTIVE	To determine the past and current psychological factors associated with slow and normal transit constipation.
POPULATION	Twenty-eight consecutive patients referred for biofeedback treatment were interviewed before the procedure.
POPULATION	All were women.
POPULATION	Transit studies revealed that 12 had slow transit constipation (STC) and 16 had normal transit constipation (NTC).
METHODS	Patients were assessed for evidence of previous and current psychiatric diagnoses using a standardized diagnostic interview schedule.
METHODS	A full family and social history was noted.
METHODS	Self-rating scales were used to measure psychological distress, abnormal attitudes to eating and current psychosocial functioning.
RESULTS	The mean age of the 28 patients was 38.2 years (SD = 10.8) with a mean duration of symptoms of 17.5 years (SD = 16.9).
RESULTS	Seventeen (61%) had a current psychiatric disorder and 18 (64%) a previous episode of psychiatric illness.
RESULTS	The mean age of the 16 NTC patients was 38.4 years (SD = 10.1) with a mean duration of symptoms of 12.4 years (SD = 15.9).
RESULTS	By contrast, the 12 STC patients had a much longer mean duration of constipation (24.3 years; SD = 16.4), a mean age of 37.9 years (SD = 12.1), with half having an onset in childhood.
RESULTS	The STC patients reported more psychosocial distress on the rating scales than those with NTC, and only one did not experience some form of adverse life event or gynaecological procedure in the 6 months before the onset of constipation.
RESULTS	Eleven (39%) of the 28 women had had a hysterectomy at a mean age of 36 years, but only four (14%) reported a history of sexual abuse.
RESULTS	Of the nine (32%) patients who reported markedly distorted attitudes to food, six had NTC and three had STC.
CONCLUSIONS	Of consecutive patients undergoing psychological assessment for intractable constipation, three fifths had evidence of current, and two thirds a previous, affective disorder.
CONCLUSIONS	One third reported distorted attitudes to food.
CONCLUSIONS	Although STC is a chronic disorder accompanied by high rates of psychological distress, it does not appear to be associated with gross functional impairment.
CONCLUSIONS	We suggest that patients who present to surgical departments with chronic intractable constipation should routinely have a psychological assessment.

###11204687
OBJECTIVE	We report the successful use of a Furlow palatoplasty to salvage velopharyngeal competence following iatrogenic avulsion of a pharyngeal flap that had been previously established to treat velopharyngeal insufficiency associated with a submucous cleft palate.
INTERVENTION	A tonsillectomy, conducted by a surgeon unaffiliated with a cleft palate team, was used to remove enlarged tonsils that had developed after pharyngeal flap surgery and extended into the lateral ports causing nasal obstruction and hypernasality because of mechanical interference with port closure.
INTERVENTION	A posttonsillectomy evaluation revealed avulsion of the pharyngeal flap, which was successfully treated using a Furlow palatoplasty.
CONCLUSIONS	To our knowledge, this is the first report of iatrogenic avulsion of a pharyngeal flap caused by tonsillectomy.
CONCLUSIONS	Based on a review of the literature and this case experience, we would conclude that tonsillectomy should not be regarded as a routine procedure in patients previously treated with a pharyngeal flap.
CONCLUSIONS	If required, it should be performed by a skilled otolaryngologist, preferably one affiliated with a multidisciplinary cleft palate team who is familiar with pharyngoplasty surgery.
CONCLUSIONS	Finally, our experience would suggest that the Furlow palatoplasty is sufficiently robust to be used as a secondary salvage procedure to restore velopharyngeal sufficiency following iatrogenic avulsion of a pharyngeal flap.

###11204685
OBJECTIVE	The purpose of this investigation was to study the adjustment and learning characteristics of children with different types of clefts.
OBJECTIVE	The hypotheses were that there may be different relationships among cleft variables (speech and appearance) according to the cleft types.
METHODS	The study compared three cleft groups on behavior rating, anxiety scales, depression scales, and self-perception (analyses of variance) and examined the influence of facial and speech ratings on self-perception (multiple regression analyses).
METHODS	All patients were treated at a university hospital cleft palate clinic.
POPULATION	Sixty-five children aged 8 years to 17 years were selected based on nonsyndromic cleft (unilateral cleft lip and palate [ULP], bilateral cleft lip and palate [BLP], and cleft palate only [CPO]) and no significant neurological condition or hearing loss.
RESULTS	The findings indicated children with CPO showed greater problems with parent- and teacher-reported depression, anxiety, and learning related to speech than children with ULP or BLP.
RESULTS	The later two groups showed fewer problems and a greater relationship of problem to facial appearance.
RESULTS	The children with ULP self-reported lower levels of depression than the other two groups.
CONCLUSIONS	Children with cleft show relatively good overall adjustment, but some problems appear related to speech and facial appearance.
CONCLUSIONS	Subgroups may need to be studied separately.

###11204684
OBJECTIVE	This study examined the ability of speech-language pathologists to transcribe compensatory articulation errors.
METHODS	Speech-language pathologists phonetically transcribed audiorecordings of 130 monosyllabic words, 70 of which contained compensatory articulations.
POPULATION	The participants for this study were two groups of 10 speech-language pathologists.
POPULATION	Group I included speech-language pathologists who were experienced in evaluating children with cleft palate, and group II speech-language pathologists were not.
RESULTS	Marked variability was evident across listeners, with percentages of agreement ranging from 19 to 71 (mean agreement = 41%).
RESULTS	The experienced listeners performed significantly better on the transcription task than the inexperienced listeners, but poor interjudge agreement was evident across both groups.
CONCLUSIONS	The results of this study suggest that speech-language pathologists may differ in their understanding of the auditory perceptual characteristics of compensatory articulations.
CONCLUSIONS	The results underscore the need for increased training and standardization of transcription procedures.

###11204293
OBJECTIVE	To establish the factors affecting the difference between screening and home blood pressure measurements.
METHODS	A cross-sectional study.
METHODS	General population in a rural Japanese community, Ohasama, Japan.
