###10731538
OBJECTIVE	To evaluate the effect of adding E(2) to progestin supplementation during the luteal phase on pregnancy and implantation rates in patients undergoing IVF cycles.
METHODS	Prospective, randomized study.
METHODS	An IVF unit in a university hospital.
POPULATION	Patients who were undergoing IVF with controlled ovarian hyperstimulation using a GnRH analog and who had E(2)2,500 pg/dL at the time of hCG administration.
INTERVENTION	Serum concentrations of E(2) and progesterone were measured in all patients on days 7, 10, and 12 after ET.
RESULTS	The E(2) and progesterone profiles of the luteal phase and the pregnancy and implantation rates were documented.
RESULTS	Data were analyzed for the entire study population and further stratified according to the GnRH analog protocol used (short or long).
RESULTS	Significantly higher E(2) levels were found during the luteal phase in the group that received E(2) supplementation.
RESULTS	This effect was more pronounced in the patients who were treated with the long GnRH analog protocol.
RESULTS	Significantly higher pregnancy and implantation rates were recorded in the patients who received E(2) supplementation and were treated with the long GnRH analog protocol.
CONCLUSIONS	For patients who are treated with the long GnRH analog protocol for controlled ovarian hyperstimulation and for whom luteal support with hCG is contraindicated, the addition of E(2) to the progestin support regimen may have a beneficial effect on pregnancy and implantation rates.

###10731537
OBJECTIVE	To evaluate whether a combination of IUI and frozen-thawed embryo transfer (FT-ET) with ovulation induction would improve the PR in couples with unexplained infertility.
METHODS	Prospective, randomized study.
METHODS	In Vitro Fertilization Unit, Tel Aviv Sourasky Medical Center, Tel Aviv, Israel.
POPULATION	Sixty-two patients with unexplained infertility were assigned into two groups.
POPULATION	The study group was composed of 32 women (38 cycles) who received ovulation induction followed by IUI and FT-ET.
POPULATION	The control group was composed of 30 women (33 cycles) who received ovulation induction followed by FT-ET.
INTERVENTION	Clomiphene citrate (CC) and hCG, IUI, and FT-ET.
RESULTS	Pregnancy rate (PR) per cycle, PR per ET.
RESULTS	In the study group, the PR per cycle and per ET were 36.8% (14 of 38) and 40.6% (13 of 32), respectively.
RESULTS	In the control group, the PR per cycle and per ET were 12.1% (4 of 33) and 14.3% (4 of 28), respectively.
RESULTS	Statistically significant differences were found between the two groups in the PR per cycle (P=.02) and PR per ET (P=.03).
RESULTS	No statistically significant difference was found between the groups for the stage in which the embryos were cryopreserved, the survival cleavage rates after thawing, grading of thawed embryos, and number of embryos transferred.
CONCLUSIONS	In couples with unexplained infertility, the PR may be improved by combining IUI and FT-ET with ovulation induction.
CONCLUSIONS	Performing IUI before thawing may prevent treatment cancellation in cycles with no surviving embryos.

###10731536
OBJECTIVE	Economic analyses in reproductive medicine often fail to take into account the chances of treatment-independent conception.
OBJECTIVE	We compared the cost-effectiveness of several realistic strategies involving IVF using no treatment as the reference strategy.
METHODS	A decision tree was constructed for a subfertile couple in which the clinician had to decide whether to offer treatment with IVF.
METHODS	No treatment at all was used as the reference strategy.
METHODS	An analytic decision-making framework.
POPULATION	Hypothetical subfertile couples.
INTERVENTION	Two potential treatment approaches: three IVF cycles performed as soon as possible, or no treatment performed initially and then three or four IVF cycles performed if a pregnancy resulting in a live birth does not occur naturally after 212 years.
RESULTS	The cost of establishing one pregnancy that results in a live birth.
RESULTS	The cost-effectiveness of IVF depended not only on the costs and expected success rates of IVF itself but also on the couple-specific chances of treatment-independent conception.
RESULTS	Consequently, the cost-effectiveness of IVF is strongly dependent on the age of the female partner.
RESULTS	This finding corresponds with everyday clinical experience.
CONCLUSIONS	Economic analyses in reproductive medicine should take into account the option of providing no treatment.

###10731535
OBJECTIVE	To compare IVF and pregnancy outcomes before and after anovulation induced by oral contraceptives.
METHODS	Observational clinical study.
METHODS	Infertility clinic.
POPULATION	Forty women with two intact ovaries (32 of 40 couples with male factor infertility and 8 with unknown causes of infertility) underwent 190 IVF treatment cycles (55 natural cycles and 135 clomiphene citrate-stimulated cycles).
INTERVENTION	If the women failed to conceive after 2-4 IVF treatment cycles, oral contraceptives were used to induce anovulation for 1 month before IVF was performed in two consecutive cycles.
RESULTS	Rates of oocyte retrieval, fertilization, cleavage, preembryo formation, pregnancy, and implantation were compared before and after a period of anovulation.
RESULTS	The pregnancy rate per cycle of the first and second cycle combined (23%) and that of the second cycle alone (30%) after a period of anovulation were significantly higher than that observed before a period of anovulation (9%).
CONCLUSIONS	Anovulation induced by oral contraceptives, showing bilateral ovarian quiescence, enhances pregnancy rates in the following two menstrual cycles of IVF treatment.

###10731534
OBJECTIVE	To investigate the functional significance of CYP11alpha microsatellite polymorphism (tttta)(n) (-528 base pairs) in patients with polycystic ovary syndrome.
METHODS	Follow-up study.
METHODS	Academic research center.
POPULATION	Eighty patients and 90 controls.
INTERVENTION	Body mass indices and waist-to-hip ratios were determined.
INTERVENTION	Blood samples were obtained for DNA analysis and hormone measurements.
RESULTS	CYP11alpha marker (tttta)(n) genotyping and serum total testosterone levels.
RESULTS	All the women were assigned to one of two genotype groups: 216+ (for women who had at least one copy of high frequency allele 216 with four repeat units) or 216- (for women who did not have allele 216).
RESULTS	Fifty-nine patients (73.75%) had genotype 216+; their mean (+/-SD) total testosterone level was 78.0 +/- 19.8 ng/dL.
RESULTS	Twenty-one patients (26.25%) had genotype 216-; their mean (+/-SD) total testosterone level was 100.0 +/- 23.3 ng/dL.
RESULTS	The difference in total testosterone levels was statistically significant.
RESULTS	Seventy-eight controls (86.67%) had genotype 216+ and 12 controls (13.33%) had genotype 216-; the total testosterone levels of these two groups were similar (38.6 +/- 15.5 vs. 40.3 +/- 12.1 ng/dL).
RESULTS	The difference in genotype distribution between the women with polycystic ovary syndrome and the controls (26.25% vs. 13.33% with genotype 216-) was statistically significant.
CONCLUSIONS	CYP11alpha (tttta)(n) allelic variants were associated with both polycystic ovary syndrome and total testosterone levels in women with polycystic ovary syndrome, suggesting the existence of an epistasis phenomenon.

###10731533
OBJECTIVE	To investigate the relationship between ovarian androgen excess and impaired growth hormone (GH)/insulin-like growth factor-1 (IGF-1) axis in nonobese women with polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS).
METHODS	A prospective, controlled clinical study.
METHODS	Reproductive Endocrine Unit, Department of Obstetrics and Gynecology, Jinling Hospital, Nanjing University School of Clinical Medicine.
POPULATION	Six patients with PCOS with both clomiphene resistance and gonadotropin hyperreponsiveness and six controls with regular cycles, matched for age and body mass index (BMI).
INTERVENTION	Bilateral ovarian wedge resection (OWR) was performed to induce ovulation surgically for these refractory women with PCOS.
INTERVENTION	A GH stimulation test with oral L-dopa was arranged for controls and for patients with PCOS before and again 6 months later after OWR.
RESULTS	Plasma GH, IGF-1, FSH, LH, testosterone, androstenedione, estradiol, progesterone, prolactin, insulin, and glucose.
RESULTS	Basal levels and areas under the response curve of GH and GH-IGF-1 ratio to L-dopa were significantly lower in patients with PCOS before surgery than those of controls.
RESULTS	The OWR in patients with PCOS obviously reduced their androstenedione and testosterone levels and insulin-glucose ratios, and increased the GH and GH-IGF-1 responses to L-dopa.
CONCLUSIONS	Impaired somatotrophic axis caused by a defect in central dopaminergic activity may be responsible for severe anovulation in these women with PCOS, which could be reversed by removing excessive androgens with OWR.

###10731532
OBJECTIVE	To determine whether testosterone levels change as women with the polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS) grow older.
METHODS	A follow-up cross-sectional study of a cohort of women with PCOS identified up to 20-25 years ago.
METHODS	Women with PCOS were recruited primarily from practice records between 1970 and 1990.
METHODS	Voter registration tapes and household directories were used to identify age-, race-, and neighborhood-matched controls.
POPULATION	Eighty-four women with PCOS, 20-57 years of age, and 37 age-matched controls participating in a study of the risk for cardiovascular disease in women with PCOS.
INTERVENTION	Clinical data were collected by questionnaire and fasting blood samples were obtained randomly throughout the menstrual cycle.
RESULTS	Total and non-SHBG-bound testosterone levels.
RESULTS	Total and non-SHBG-bound testosterone levels were similar in women with PCOS who were 20-42 years of age but were reduced by approximately 50% among women 42-47 years of age and remained stable in women older than 47 years of age.
RESULTS	Testosterone levels were increased in younger and older women with PCOS compared with controls but were similar to controls in women 42-47 years of age.
CONCLUSIONS	Hyperandrogenism partly resolves before menopause in women with PCOS.
CONCLUSIONS	This change may explain the tendency of women with PCOS to cycle regularly as they grow older.
CONCLUSIONS	Testosterone levels remain elevated in older women with PCOS, however, and may contribute to their increased risk for cardiovascular disease, endometrial cancer, and other diseases.

###10731531
OBJECTIVE	To compare the clinical efficacy of an original combined therapy with cyproterone acetate, 2 mg, and ethinylestradiol, 35 microgram (Diane 35), plus finasteride (5 mg) for 2 weeks per month with that of Diane 35 alone in hirsute women.
METHODS	Prospective randomized, single-blinded study.
METHODS	Outpatients in an academic research environment.
POPULATION	Fifty women with idiopathic hirsutism (IH) or the polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS).
INTERVENTION	Group 1 (n = 25) received Diane 35 alone and group 2 (n = 25) received Diane 35 plus finasteride.
INTERVENTION	The latter drug was administered using a new therapeutic scheme: 14 consecutive days for each therapeutic cycle.
RESULTS	Hormonal evaluation was done before beginning treatment and after 3 and 6 months of therapy.
RESULTS	Hirsutism was graded at 3-month intervals.
RESULTS	The combination of Diane 35 plus finasteride for 14 days significantly decreased the hirsutism score after 3 months of therapy, while Diane 35 alone induced this effect after 6 months.
CONCLUSIONS	Finasteride in combination with Diane 35 for 14 days is effective, well accepted, and safe in hirsute patients, as the amount of antiandrogenic drugs administered is much lower than that in conventional treatment.

###10731530
OBJECTIVE	To evaluate the influence of the opioid system on the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis in women with polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS).
METHODS	Controlled clinical study.
METHODS	Academic research environment.
POPULATION	Eight lean and 12 obese women with PCOS, and seven lean and 5 obese control subjects.
INTERVENTION	Each patient received an i.v. bolus of naloxone at a dose of 125 microgram per kilogram of body weight; 48 hours later, each patient received 16 mg of loperamide p.o.
RESULTS	Samples were collected for 2 hours for the naloxone test and for 3 hours for the loperamide test.
RESULTS	Levels of adrenocorticotropic hormone (ACTH) and cortisol were measured in all plasma samples.
RESULTS	The obese women with PCOS had a greater ACTH and cortisol response to opiate blockade than either the lean women with PCOS or the control subjects, but there was no difference between the lean or obese control subjects and the lean women with PCOS.
RESULTS	There was no difference in the responsiveness of the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis to loperamide between the PCOS and control groups.
CONCLUSIONS	The data indicate that the sensitivity of the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis to opioids cannot be altered in women with PCOS.
CONCLUSIONS	However, abnormalities of the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis in women with PCOS could be central in origin, as suggested by the effects of naloxone administration, and probably are related to the anthropometric characteristics of these hyperandrogenic patients.

###10731529
OBJECTIVE	Determination of telomerase activity and the expression of human telomerase RNA (hTR) and human telomerase reverse transcriptase (hTERT) in the testicular tissue of patients with infertility arising from various causes.
METHODS	Prospective observational study.
METHODS	A university hospital.
POPULATION	Thirty-three patients with azoospermia arising from various causes.
POPULATION	There were 12 testicular biopsy specimens from patients with Sertoli cell-only syndrome, 9 from patients with maturation arrest, and 12 from patients with obstructive azoospermia and normal histologic findings.
INTERVENTION	Thirty-three testicular biopsies.
RESULTS	Correlation of histologic findings at testicular biopsy with telomerase activity, hTERT, and hTR.
RESULTS	All 12 biopsy specimens from patients with obstructive azoospermia were positive for telomerase activity, hTR, and hTERT.
RESULTS	Biopsy specimens from the 9 patients with maturation arrest were positive for telomerase activity in 8 cases, hTR in 9 cases, and hTERT in 5 cases.
RESULTS	None of the patients with Sertoli cell-only syndrome showed either telomerase activity or hTERT, but all of them showed hTR.
CONCLUSIONS	Telomerase activity and evidence of hTERT in testicular tissue are highly sensitive and highly specific markers of gametogenesis, which could gain in importance as part of the fertility workup before microinjection procedures.

###10731528
OBJECTIVE	To evaluate a possible involvement of the extracellular matrix (ECM) in the testes of adolescents with varicocele.
METHODS	Cross-sectional study.
METHODS	University-based medical center.
POPULATION	Twenty-four adolescents aged between 13 and 18 years underwent surgical treatment for repair of left idiopathic varicocele.
INTERVENTION	A testis biopsy was performed at time of surgery.
RESULTS	Transmission electron microscopy study of basal lamina and immunofluorescence studies of collagen type IV and laminin, two major components of basal lamina.
RESULTS	Transmission electron microscopy observations showed an uneven profile of the basal lamina with a variable thickness.

RESULTS	Collagen type IV showed some areas of strong immunostaining with other areas with reduced immunoreactivity.

###10731527
OBJECTIVE	To compare the empty zona pellucidae (ZP) of different species for use in the cryopreservation of spermatozoa.
METHODS	Prospective study.
METHODS	An IVF unit of a medical center.
POPULATION	Patients with azoospermia, oligoasthenozoospermia, or normal spermatozoa.
INTERVENTION	Human and mouse ZP were prepared by evacuating the cytoplasm of oocytes or embryos.
INTERVENTION	The evacuated ZP were injected with spermatozoa from patients with severe oligoasthenozoospermia and from healthy, fertile men.
INTERVENTION	After the freezing and thawing procedure, the spermatozoa were aspirated outside the ZP.
RESULTS	The number of spermatozoa per ZP, the number of motile sperm before freezing and after thawing, the number of sperm lost per ZP after freezing, and the sperm recovery rate were compared according to the different origins of the ZP and the sperm.
RESULTS	The number of spermatozoa, number of motile sperm before freezing and after thawing, number of sperm lost per ZP, and sperm recovery rate were comparable in all groups.
RESULTS	The total mean number of motile sperm before freezing and after thawing, the mean number of nonmotile sperm after thawing, the mean number of sperm lost after thawing, and the sperm recovery rate were 14.5%, 11. 8%, 1.0%, 1.5%, and 82%, respectively.
CONCLUSIONS	Zona pellucidae are an ideal vehicle for the cryopreservation of sperm collected by testicular sperm extraction or microsurgical epididymal sperm aspiration or from patients with severe oligoasthenozoospermia.
CONCLUSIONS	There were no differences when human and mouse ZP were used for sperm storage.

###10731526
OBJECTIVE	To determine the rate at which mild acidity immobilizes and kills human sperm and to evaluate an acidic microbicide, BufferGel, for sperm immobilization.
METHODS	Controlled in vitro study.
METHODS	An academic research university and hospital andrology lab.
POPULATION	Eight volunteer male sperm donors.
INTERVENTION	Semen samples were treated with hydrochloric acid (HCl) or BufferGel.
RESULTS	Sperm motility was measured by using a computerized automated semen analyzer and video microscopy.
RESULTS	Sperm membrane permeability and intracellular pH were measured by using fluorescent techniques.
RESULTS	In semen acidified with HCl to pH 4.0, sperm were rapidly immobilized (within 1 min) and were irreversibly immobilized (killed) within 10 minutes.
RESULTS	The speed of immobilization and of killing were both linearly proportional to hydrogen ion activity over a pH range of 7.5-4.0.
RESULTS	Across the same range, the intracellular pH of human sperm equilibrated to within 0.5 pH units of extracellular pH within 1-2 minutes.
RESULTS	BufferGel immobilized sperm significantly faster than HCl from pH 4.0-6.0.
CONCLUSIONS	Exposure to mild acidity rapidly acidifies the intracellular pH of human sperm and is rapidly spermicidal.
CONCLUSIONS	BufferGel accelerates acid immobilization of sperm.

###10731525
OBJECTIVE	To investigate the capacitation and motility kinetics of spermatozoa treated with human follicular fluid (FF).
METHODS	Controlled, experimental laboratory study.
METHODS	University-based gynecology unit.
POPULATION	Human FF was collected from women undergoing assisted reproductive treatment.
POPULATION	Semen samples were obtained from men visiting subfertility clinics.
INTERVENTION	Spermatozoa were incubated with human FF under various experimental conditions.
INTERVENTION	Spermatozoa incubated with Earle's balanced salt solution were used as the control.
RESULTS	Chlortetracycline staining patterns and sperm motility parameters.
RESULTS	The rate of capacitation in the human FF-treated spermatozoa was significantly higher than that in the control spermatozoa after 1 hour and 3 hours of treatment.
RESULTS	The percentage of acrosome-reacted spermatozoa also was significantly higher after human FF treatment than after control treatment.
RESULTS	These effects of human FF were dose-dependent.
RESULTS	Human FF-treated spermatozoa maintained their velocities at the zero-hour level for 5 hours, whereas the velocities of the control spermatozoa decreased significantly after 1 hour.
RESULTS	Human FF treatment significantly increased the beat cross-frequency above the rate at zero hour for 5 hours.
RESULTS	The hyperactivation of the human FF-treated spermatozoa remained stable for 3 hours, whereas that of the control spermatozoa decreased significantly after 1 hour of incubation.
RESULTS	Significantly more human FF-treated spermatozoa underwent hyperactivation than did control spermatozoa after 1 hour and 3 hours of treatment.
RESULTS	The effects of human FF on beat cross-frequency and hyperactivation were dose-dependent.
CONCLUSIONS	Human FF promotes capacitation and the acrosome reaction within a short period.
CONCLUSIONS	It also stimulates or maintains various sperm motility parameters.

###10731523
OBJECTIVE	To assess the efficacy of various controlled ovarian hyperstimulation (COH) regimens in the prior poor-responder patient preparing for assisted reproductive techniques.
METHODS	English-language literature review.
POPULATION	Candidates for assisted reproductive techniques who had been defined as having a prior suboptimal response to standard COH regimens.
INTERVENTION	A variety of regimes are reviewed, including increased gonadotropin doses, change of gonadotropins, adjunctive growth hormone (GH), luteal phase (long) GnRH agonist (GnRH-a) initiation, early follicular phase (flare) GnRH-a initiation, low-dose luteal phase (ultrashort) GnRH-a initiation, progestin pretreatment, and microdose flare GnRH-a initiation.
RESULTS	Maximal serum E(2) levels, follicular development, dose, and duration of gonadotropin therapy, cycle cancellation rates, oocytes retrieved, embryos transferred, and clinical and ongoing pregnancy rates.
RESULTS	A lack of uniformity in definition of the poor responder and of prospective randomized trials make data interpretation somewhat difficult.
RESULTS	Of the varied strategies proposed, those that seem to be more uniformly beneficial are microdose GnRH-a flare and late luteal phase initiation of a short course of low-dose GnRH-a discontinued before COH.
CONCLUSIONS	No single regimen will benefit all poor responders.
CONCLUSIONS	General acceptance of uniform definitions and performance of large-scale prospective randomized trials are critical.
CONCLUSIONS	Development of a reliable precycle screen will allow effective differentiation among normal responders, poor responders, and those who will not conceive with their own oocytes.

###10730528
BACKGROUND	The intermittent intravenous cyclophosphamide in the treatment of lupus nephritis in adults is well tolerated and associated with clinical improvement and long term stability of renal function.
BACKGROUND	However, there are few reports about the efficacy of intravenous pulse cyclophosphamide (IPC) of severe lupus nephritis in children.
OBJECTIVE	To evaluate the clinical efficacy, renal function, renal outcome and complications of IPC therapy in children with severe lupus nephritis.
METHODS	Prospective study.
POPULATION	Children with severe lupus nephritis have been followed-up for at least 6 months.
POPULATION	Treatment regimen: Intravenous pulse cyclophosphamide 0.5-0.75 g/m2 given monthly for 6 months with subsequent doses given at 2-3 months interval up to 3 years and combined with low dose oral prednisolone therapy.
RESULTS	Thirty-one children (mean age: 12.31 +/- 2.03 years; female:male = 24:7) with severe lupus nephritis received IPC therapy. 24 out of 28 patients (85.7%) had diffuse proliferative glomerulonephritis.
RESULTS	After 3 months of treatment, most patients were clinically improved as evidenced by significant improvements in 24-hour urine protein, creatinine clearance, serum creatinine, BUN, serum albumin and C3 level.
RESULTS	These improvements were sustained up to 18 months and were accompanied by a significant reduction in prednisolone dosage.
RESULTS	Renal outcome at the last follow-up (range = 6-76 months) demonstrated that twelve patients (38.7%) had complete remission, 18 patients (58.0%) still had significant proteinuria and only one had serum creatinine of 1.6 mg/dl at 42 months.
RESULTS	One child progressed to end stage renal diseases during IPC therapy.
RESULTS	Five patients had severe infections during the treatment resulting in one death.
RESULTS	Hemorrhagic cystitis and malignancies were not found.
CONCLUSIONS	Treatment of severe lupus nephritis in children with intravenous pulse cyclophosphamide is associated with favorable short term results.
CONCLUSIONS	Severe infections are the major complications.

###10730170
OBJECTIVE	To assess the risk of infection of either subcutaneously implanted central venous access devices or percutaneous central venous catheters inserted via a subcutaneous tunnel in cancer patients with a positive staphylococcal nasal carriage.
METHODS	Prospective study.
POPULATION	The study included 266 patients undergoing cancer chemotherapy.
METHODS	A nasal swab was taken prior to insertion of the venous access device and the patients were followed over 30 days for the occurrence of a staphylococcal infection (hemoculture and device or site of insertion).
RESULTS	A nasal staphylococcal nasal carriage was found in 227 patients.
RESULTS	Out of the 15 developing a device infection, a staphylococcal nasal colonization was existing in nine patients.
RESULTS	Bacteriological screening a sensitivity of 60% and a specificity of 13%.
CONCLUSIONS	Bacteriological screening at the time of device insertion of a central venous access device is of no value for the detection of patients at risk of staphylococcal infection of the device.

###10727696
BACKGROUND	Lobar atrophy is an important neuroimaging feature of Pick complex (PiC).
BACKGROUND	However, differences in patterns of focal brain atrophy between PiC and Alzheimer's disease (AD), and among PiC subgroups, have not been studied quantitatively.
OBJECTIVE	To compare volumetric measures among primary progressive aphasia (PPA), frontotemporal dementia (FTD) and AD; to assess association between brain atrophy and cognition.
POPULATION	Seventeen patients with PPA, 11 with FTD and 24 with probable AD were studied.
METHODS	We measured total and regional volume quantitatively using MRI and computerized volumetry.
METHODS	Contributing factors were controlled statistically or by adopting brain volume ratios.
METHODS	We investigated the classifying power of volumetry and correlated regional brain volume with cognitive and language test scores.
RESULTS	The ratio for fronto-temporo-central region was smaller on the left in PPA and on the right in FTD.
RESULTS	AD and some PPA patients had smaller parietal lobes.
RESULTS	The frontal ratios correctly classified 93% of PPA and FTD patients, but only 50% of the entire PiC and AD patients.
RESULTS	Language-dependent examinations correlated with the left fronto-temporal volume.
CONCLUSIONS	Brain atrophy differs in PPA, FTD and AD, but there is some morphological overlap between PiC and AD in parietal volumes.
CONCLUSIONS	Focal brain atrophy is most consistently associated with language impairments.

###10724081
OBJECTIVE	To investigate the value of the "tethered" median nerve stress test (TMST) in predicting electrodiagnostically confirmed carpal tunnel syndrome (CTS) in patients with symptoms suggestive of CTS.
METHODS	Blinded comparison of a clinical diagnostic test with neurophysiologic testing.
METHODS	Portland (OR) Veterans Administration Medical Center Electrodiagnostic Laboratory.
POPULATION	One hundred two consecutive patients referred for symptoms suggestive of CTS.
POPULATION	Study inclusion criteria were referral for evaluation of symptoms of paresthesia (with or without pain) inclusive of the median nerve distribution distal to the wrist.
RESULTS	Fifty-seven percent of referred patients had electrodiagnostically confirmed CTS.
RESULTS	The sensitivity of the TMST was 50%.
RESULTS	The specificity was 59.1%.
RESULTS	The positive predictive value was 61.7%.
RESULTS	The negative predictive value was 47.3%.
CONCLUSIONS	The TMST does not have utility in predicting electrodiagnostic consult results in veteran patients with symptoms suggestive of CTS.

###10724073
OBJECTIVE	To examine the long-term outcomes of persons undergoing trauma-related amputations, and to explore factors affecting their physical, social, and mental health and the role of inpatient rehabilitation in improving such outcomes.
METHODS	Abstracted medical records and interview data sought for a retrospective cohort of persons who had undergone a lower-limb trauma-related amputation.
POPULATION	Patients identified with a principal or secondary diagnosis of a trauma-related amputation to the lower extremity at the University of Maryland Shock Trauma Center between 1984 and 1994.
POPULATION	Patients with spinal cord injury or traumatic brain injury were excluded.
RESULTS	Of 146 patients who had trauma-related amputations to the lower limb at the University of Maryland Shock Trauma Center during the study period, nearly 9% died during the acute admission and 3.5% died after discharge.
RESULTS	About 87% of all trauma-related amputations involved males, and roughly three quarters involved white persons.
RESULTS	About 80% of all amputations occurred before age 40.
RESULTS	The health profile of traumatic amputee subjects interviewed in the study (n = 78, 68% response rate) was systematically lower than that of the general US population for all SF-36 scores.
RESULTS	The differences in profiles were largest among SF-36 scales sensitive to differences in physical health status, particularly physical functioning, role limitations due to physical health, and bodily pain.
RESULTS	About one fourth of persons with a trauma-related amputation reported ongoing severe problems with the residual limb, including phantom pain, wounds, and sores.
RESULTS	The number of inpatient rehabilitation nights significantly improved the ability of patients with amputation to function in their physical roles, increased vitality, and reduced bodily pain.
RESULTS	Inpatient rehabilitation was also significantly correlated with improved vocational outcomes.
CONCLUSIONS	These findings suggest a substantial effect of inpatient rehabilitation in improving long-term outcomes of persons with trauma-related amputations.

###10722014
OBJECTIVE	To evaluate the benefits and difficulties encountered when using various 3-dimensional (3-D) navigation systems in head and neck procedures.
METHODS	Five different navigation systems were used for preoperative planning and intraoperative 3-D navigation in procedures at the paranasal sinuses, the frontal and lateral skull bases, and the petrous bone.
INTERVENTION	Intraoperative 3-D localizing systems (position-sensitive mechanical arms, infrared cameras, etc) demand reliable patient fixation on the operating table.
INTERVENTION	We achieved this by developing a noninvasive head holder.
INTERVENTION	Other systems allow patient movements by using magnetic digitizing technology (ARTMA System) and sophisticated programming.
RESULTS	Having surpassed an initial learning curve, we now achieve an accuracy of 1 to 2 mm regularly.
RESULTS	Especially in paranasal and frontal basal surgery, all navigation systems used provide valuable positioning information during surgery.
RESULTS	In particular for revision or tumor surgery, decisive benefits resulted from use of these systems: shorter overall operation time; safer manipulation near delicate structures; and reliable identification of the skull base even in patients with bleeding, scarring, or missing anatomical landmarks.
CONCLUSIONS	We performed approximately 250 operations with different systems and introduced navigation at the lateral skull base and the petrous bone with mechanical, optic, and magnetic digitizers.
CONCLUSIONS	In these anatomical areas, navigation was used successfully; the technical challenge is greatest at the lateral skull base, however.

###10722010
OBJECTIVE	To evaluate the diagnostic efficacy of computed tomography (CT)-guided needle biopsies of head and neck lesions.
METHODS	All CT-guided needle biopsies of head and neck lesions performed between September 1994 and February 1999 were included.
METHODS	Cytopathologic and histologic records, along with patient clinical records, were reviewed.
METHODS	A tertiary care medical center.
POPULATION	Patients referred for evaluation of lesions inaccessible to routine methods of needle biopsy.
RESULTS	Thirty-seven patients underwent 42 CT-guided biopsies.
RESULTS	There were included 12 lesions in or adjacent to the skull base and 9 lesions around the pharyngoesophageal or laryngotracheal complex; the other lesions were located in the deep lobe of the parotid gland (n = 7), deep neck area (n = 12), and thyroid gland (n = 2).
RESULTS	Diagnostic cytologic biopsy specimens were obtained in 38 (91%) of 42 needle biopsy procedures.
RESULTS	The results were supported histologically and/or clinically in 36 cases (95%).
RESULTS	Eighteen patients underwent open surgical procedures.
RESULTS	Histologic confirmation was found in 86% of cases.
RESULTS	Nineteen patients (51%) avoided an open surgical procedure: 11 with benign disease and 8 with recurrent malignancy.
RESULTS	There were no false-positive or false-negative results, and no complications were identified.
CONCLUSIONS	Computed tomography-guided needle biopsy is a safe and reliable minimally invasive technique for the diagnosis of poorly accessible or deep-seated lesions of the head and neck.
CONCLUSIONS	Diagnostic needle biopsies allow improved preoperative planning and patient counseling in surgical patients and avoidance of open surgical procedures in patients with benign disease or recurrent malignant neoplasms.

###10722001
